### Sean Waters — 2018-08-01T10:34:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-247179>

I think i'm on the right track........

I hear the CT stuff and Deuterium skin pinch effect, and it sounds like really cool Laser Science. But what does that translate to in simple practical terms?

Sunbathing and Ice Baths.

I've done both, and neither had the key to the door of Fatigue and Eczema.

In June i did a CT protocol for 4 weeks and got to 51 F for 60 minutes, no shivering. It didn't help the rash i'm afraid, if anything I would wake up in the night and scratch more. I know from your perspectives, this just couldn't make sense, but that's what happened.

In addition, back in Nov/Dec 17 when i first got really unwell, I did a month of CT back then in 45 F water - DEEP CT. It seemed to clear up initially and look better from the outside, but then it itched like fuck and woke me up during my sleep, contributing to my morning fatigue, and would be back scabby and sore within a day or 2 of no CT.

So that says, yes it works short term, but is it a permanent cure? Nope!

**_This isn't the only thread where someone says "Im doing CT and getting lots of sun but my rash won't go away" either people..... Doc always said, Skin = Vit A cycle in brain (SLEEP)._**

**What is the only thing that stops scratching of a night time?

Pre-9pm Early Bedtime.

Once again, last night i was asleep by 8.30, and I had no scratching in my sleep. 3 days in a row....**

### kris90 — 2018-08-01T14:21:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-247189>

Cold showers definitely count. I take 2 a day year round, but I do tubs and snow bathing in winter, as well as spend a lot of time outdoors in -30 C in our Canadian winters.

Here's a couple things to note:
\-Cooling skin surface = increase electron density = have more magnetic "pull" for sunlight on your skin (DHA helps with this too because of its electron density)
\-Cooling skin surface not only increases our "pull" for sunlight, but it also increases heat flow from deep in our tissues (mitochondrial uncoupling). So you have heat flowing upward from tissues into blood, and heat coming down (from the sun - i.e. UVA light) and that puts the pinch on deuterium

^ That is how it works for a loosely coupled haplotype. There's more magnetism involved, because the electric fields from the sun are weaker at higher latitudes. For a tightly coupled haplotype, they have strong UV/IR light year round, so again, the UV light presses down on skin, the IR light gets absorbed in water of tissues, and the heat rises up. Water is basically a mirror for IR light, so that's how the Africans get their pinch from UV/IR and they don't need to release heat from mitochondria.

So we need to use cold to increase our magnetic field to get more driving force from the sun on our surface, and release more heat from our tissues to make the pinch.

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-09T10:41:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-247990>

Update -

So I've had about 7 out of the last 8 days with sleep before 9pm - and all those days I've not scratched; the day I got to sleep @ 9.30pm I had a midnight flare up.

Everyday my eczema clears just a little bit more. My hands feels smooth now, but they still look a bit dry and red. Not quite there yet.

I've had it since a baby, but the hand rash has been here about 18 months. So i've got a lot of tissue damage to recover. I'm hoping in the next month or two that i can fully heal the tissue by regeneration in sleep.

One thing I've noticed, I can't sleep @ night before 9pm if i've had any carbs or fruits or any kind of "irritant" @ lunch or dinner. I can have them for breakfast, but no other time. They seem to cause a bit of inflammation in my gut, which raises Cortisol, and that spike keeps it trending higher at a later time, when it should be very low. So i'm very "wakeful" and "alert" @ 8pm. Without them (the last 5 days), I've found i have no issues dropping off @ 8pm or 8.30pm. I have however, been waking @ 1am and 3am, I'm still trying to hack that, but at least my skin is healing!

I've also cut the carbs and fruit in the morning (sorry [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ) , and i've started eating 5 Oysters per day (smaller ones than the picture earlier in the journal!).

The impact this (cutting fruits altogether) has had on my libido has been immense. I think any fruits or irritants = inflammation (for me) in my gut, due to my history of IBD/Crohn's this would make sense. So the Pregnenolone is shunted to Cortisol for the anti-inflammatory affect on the gut, which leaves my Sex Steroids and Progesterone in a dump.

This all makes sense, if you see my recent lab tests = low free T and very low Progesterone (no pregnenolone to make them, shunted to Cortisol) and high HS-CRP (Inflammation raising the Cortisol).

So my Libido is literally firing up - I'm waking up horny and going to bed horny. This is something I never paid attention to in my wellness, i thought I was fine, but now I realise I wasn't at all!

One thing I find, is that without Carbs in the AM, just eggs + seaweed + onion + oysters and coconut oil, that I can get dehydrated and feel a bit of a lag by the Strong AM Sun we have here at the moment and the heat (and of course the EMF). This used to be my BIGGEST problem. I went to Bali and tried to do Keto in 35'C heat and just got so fatigued it ruined the trip, this was the story in Mexico too - which is why i started using the Salt hack. **The Sun deactivates Adrenaline, so if you are a shit Cortisol receptor or Low Cortisol then you will feel tired. This is the story of Mexico for me: I had terrible Cortisol + so I felt worse and more fatigued in the Sun. And Why i feel better in the UK when i got home and did the Leptin Rx, because it's focus is reducing Cortisol to reset communication with Hypothalamus and peripheral energy status.**

This lag it's only slight, barely noticeable, which shows how much my energy and metabolism has improved since Mexico and Bali. Still, I'm striving for more and I sense a little bit of Adrenal Fatigue just lingering... So i'm hoping, in a month, once the inflammation is right down and my sleep is perfect - that i won't get this "lag" at all. Alternatively, I will go BACK on the Leptin Reset to finish the job for another month or two. No problems there.

Today, it has rained in the morning in London for first time in about 3 months, it is a lot cooler, and I feel VERY energetic after my zero carb breakfast and no "lag" whatsoever. This gives me a lot of excitement for Winter - because if I can't win with the Sleep Hack this summer - then I know that CT this winter will absolutely sky rocket my wellness anyway.

I've smoke weed as a youth for a few years, but not had any drugs in a few years now.

Thought it may be Olive Oil (Arachidonic Acid is very similar shape to the Cannabinoid Receptors), or Organic Berries (maybe some pesticide or toxin), but i've cut both of those out now. It seems to be just consistent with when i eat DHA.

Low testosterone and high estrogens promote arterial plaque buildup.
progesterone have some 5aR inhibitory ability.
You do not want more than (top laboratory level progesterone)(1.4ng/mL)
But less than that is not better. (P/E ratio)

Testosterone is converted to 5a-DHT with assistance of 5aR enzyme.
Good levels of 5a-DHT promote erections, libido.

Many foods that we eat are 5aR inhibitors (bad for sex, sometimes good for other health benefits).

Possibly the foods that you dropped were in category of 5aR inhibitors.

**List of 5α-reductase inhibitors**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List\_of\_5α-reductase\_inhibitors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_5%CE%B1-reductase_inhibitors)

**Herbs and other inhibitors**

..............

Interesting short list (fatty acids):
The relative inhibitory potencies of unsaturated fatty acids are, in decreasing order:
Gamma-Linolenic acid,
alpha-linolenic acid,
linoleic acid,
palmitoleic acid,
oleic acid, and
myristoleic acid.\[43\]

................

I have made it Blog number **#134 EMF 2: Einstein Meet Leptin.**

**How might memory really work?**
Science does not know how memory works. I think I do. Here is why: the human brain can also slow the
charge, current, or electron flow, using alterations in cerebral auto-regulation to different areas of the brain.
It can also decrease conductivity by changing the amount of CSF in the head based upon circadian cycles.
This is often why pseudo tumor cerebri is found in over weight females. This is due to electron loss and and
increase of CSF production by an altered clock. We can see radical improvements in their headache, if we
alter their chronobiology first. No one does, but I did, and it and it works. By decreasing blood flow, using
higher levels of CO2 by hyper-ventiltion, and increases in eNOS in the blood, the brain dramatically
decrease cerebral blood flow in parts of the Circle of Willis and the circulatory system around our
hippocampus to allow us to access memories of yester-years in a previous time. **By modulating the
electron flow in the mitochondria of neurons, we can go back in time, to an older reality.** This, I believe, is
the basis for human memory and our unique ability to plan for change to change our own reality, in my
opinion.

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-13T11:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248343>

Went home for the weekend, Friday and Saturday night, back at my Moms house in the countryside.

Still got all the wifi and alexa and bad lights etc. Haven't fully convinced her about Blue light + EMF yet!

But I noted the Best Sleep i've had in months..... I slept from 8.30pm until 4.30am straight through, 8 hours solid hours. Again, no scratching. The Saturday night i did similar but woke a little earlier and snoozed in a little later. Again, no scratching.

But what was different was that as well as the Absence of a "flare up" or scratching of the skin, I actually HEALED the skin even more than I do in London when I sleep before 9. It wasn't Dry when i woke up, it looked completely normal, even the redness wasn't there. My Mom was absolutely astonished. That much so, that she has decided to give the "Why we sleep?" book a read and wants to re-arrange her schedule to sleep before 9.

Another thing was my libido and morning glory as we call it in the UK (is that a term in the US?) - well anyway, the libido was the best it's been so far. I also relaxed a huge amount, when i arrived friday night to when i left sunday afternoon I had completely relaxed and I felt really really good, despite it raining all weekend!

I get back to my mouldy london house and sleep from 8.30 til 2.30am wake up, then snooze til 5am. Why can't I sleep straight through here? The likely culprit is the nnEMF. But I also think it is the Mould (which proliferates uncontrollably under high nnEMF).

I had no scratching, but there it was again this morning - the dryness on my hands. So my hands are now more dehydrated from a night sleep in this house, than just the day before in my Mom's house.

Can't wait to move out - 6 weeks!!

The final thing i've found, since this Reset back in May, i've LOST SO MUCH WEIGHT. And since i've got the sleep down before 9, and my hunger is controlled, i'm literally zero % body fat. I look Lean as fuck, but a little too skinny for my liking. I've really tried to battle with the illusion of Mens Magazines/ Bodybuilding websites that i need to look like Arnie. But I'm also aware that i can just be "too thin", and that it might indicate an issue in the body. "Skinny LR folks" Jack calls them in his early blogs.

He says, "eat more raw cream and berries, cover your hash browns in butter" for the skinny LR guys. Well, I shouldn't be LR anymore, my labs all indicate a perfect thyroid and my appetite has gone and i feel energy, no adrenal fatigue, and i don't get crashes after food (IR). But I haven't added that muscle mass yet.

I hate talking about carbs and fats and calories. With all we know in this forum, i try to think of things more simply - in terms of the physics or thermodynamics. In one sense, I have condensed my matter and more Light = less Mass on the E=MC2 reversed equation. So being this skinny/ lean hunter gatherer type body is a good sign? But can't skinny bodies also be reflective of not enough light too? Like Anorexia, is surely lack of light, it's the oppposite form to obesity.

My non-kruse friends and family just keep saying "Exercise" and "weight lifting" - but i know that's not the answer. It always has to be SIMPLE.

Part of me believes it is my sleep - as Growth Hormone at 3-5am is where we grow and build muscle whilst burning fat. In London, as i said above, i usually wake at 2 or 3am and light sleep until 4 or 5am. Maybe this part of my sleep, which was so efficient at my Moms house where i slept through, is where i'm losing the ability to Grow and build muscle?

Maybe the eczema is controlled by the earlier phases of sleep, 8.30pm til 1/2am, where im out cold and deep in sleep - hence the eczema recovery. And the muscle building/growth hormone stage is at 3am til 5am, where im effectively half awake and snoozing.

In the meantime, I've started eating Raw Organic Chocolate Butter + Raw Organic Cacao Paste + Raw Organic Cacao Nibs + 60g Coconut Oil + Cinnamon + Sea Salt + Nutmeg. I just melt it all in a pan and then add the Nibs and eat/drink with a spoon while still warm. It's so amazing, and it doesn't give my Gut any Stress - i show no physical signs of inflammation or irritation. So it is a perfect "Weight gainer" for a leaky gut skinny guy.

30g ROC Butter (270 kcal) + 30g ROC Paste (240 kcal) + 60g CO (540 kcal) = 1050 calories. That should be putting "weight" on to me, but if anything i've gotten THINNER since starting it! All those Electrons in Raw 100% Chocolate making my mass decrease even more?? C2 increases, M decreases and E remains contant, right?

Once a week i come home to her crying. It scares me! Her best friend has been trying to discover what the depression "is", the source. So she went to Peru on an Ayahuasca trip, Shaman and the pipes in a cave, and she's not got the answer she's looking for. She's home, and is "out of ideas" with how to tackle her depression, and "wants to die".
>
> To here this, knowing what I know, is gut wrenchingly difficult.
>
> I showed my close friend a documentary called "magic pill" about the ketogenic diet - i know we are ahead of that on this site, but i thought i'd get her ROS down and increase Electron flow down her membranes, lol. She bought it, and she's gone keto, and had some of the best weeks of her life - however, she doesn't journal, so when she crashes off a sugar binge or alcohol binge, she falls into a pit of hopelessness, as she doesn't understand - and believes keto to be failing her, when really she's failing keto!!
>
> The sugar cravings and alcohol and substance abuse that she partakes in are all due to Leptin resistance and therefore circadian mismatch. She sits on a Macbook til late at night. She also says "i sleep for hours, but still feel tired, i'm always so so tired!!".
>
> So, i though, i will show her the leptin reset. she eats a BAB, and feels better - no cravings.
>
> But again, she fails after a week and collapses again.
>
> then, i thought i would try to convince her of blue light and sleep..... i give her the Why We Sleep book, she reads 1 chapter, and fails to finish.
>
> I keep trying all the time, but the fact is - she is so low dopamine that nothing will stick, she can't keep a routine for more than a week. It's reciprocal behavior.
>
> Now she's talking of going to the doctor, for the same meds that have never helped her..... It's so hard sometimes!!!

Hey Sean, nice updates! I probably should be more reserved in my ‘liking’ of posts but I can’t help it.

I never seriously considered suicide, but let’s just say I pondered theoretically, with morbid curiosity. Sleep is the critical factor me. In almost every aspect of my biology, i'm finding, that Sleep is the dominant factor in optimal function.

No amount of oysters or anything will get my libido up if i've had a bad sleep.

.... and on that note, I write this after having a terrible night's sleep. And literally got no brain function today, no libido or wood this morning, i'm overly emotional and I've had bad eczema all night.

and I've had 5 oysters for breakfast - and nope, still feel like shit.

I'm considering arranging to move out my house this month, instead of next month. The reason I want to stay is because i've been able to sleep before 9pm and even though i have wake ups through the night in this house, the early bedtime is almost "protective" of those disruptions, and i wake up feeling fine. However, if i miss that 9pm window, then i'm subject to a night of scratching and a terrible night's sleep.

At my moms house at the weekend, i slept straight through from 8.30 til 4.30 and had the best 2 days of Libido/ cognitive function/ eczema in years...

I think the Mold/EMF in this house is clearly a big obstacle for me.

However, I have the opportunity to pay off the nutrition College Course, in full for the year, at a reduced cost, in 2 weeks time with my savings. Then, I will have to save up again in September to move out end of September.

Alternatively, I move now, 4 weeks early, and i'm subject to pay the college course monthly at a higher rate.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-16T12:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248587>

[@Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/) I'm sorry to hear you are having a bad day. I have had days where I just had to push through, SO looking forward to bedtime... and knowing that after a good night of sleep, everything will be better. It sucks, but at least it gives me something to look forward to, on a day like that.

And something I've thought about many times lately ~ and I know you'll be able to relate to: I've realized that \*good days\* are actually MY NORM now, and the crappy ones are rare, which makes them that much more noticeable.

This is in contrast to living in a crappy overly-emotional keyed-up state all the time ... and having the occasional really awesome day here and there, where I thought, "man, if I could just feel this way all the time!" or "what is different about today, so I can replicate it?!"

It's flip-flopped. And THAT is HUGE.

I also do find that I can literally plug my body into the earth, barefoot in the sun, and I picture it like a phone going on its charger ~ and I've described it that way to my kids. I hope you are able to do that today, even if it's just for a few minutes at a time.

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-17T10:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248669>

Got to sleep just before 9pm - Woke @ 3am! But felt REFRESHED. Then I gave myself an extra 90 min REM cycle and got up @ 4.45am... Feel WAY better today.

No eczema, no cognition dysfunction, I happy switched on and ready to go with work today. Yesterday just seems like a bad dream...

THE POWER OF SLEEP!!!

What is significant is that I only slept 6-7 hrs total. Which is meant to be "not enough" according to conventional wisdom. But I keep reading, without any citations, "you can get all your NREM and REM sleep needs in 4 hour window between 8pm to Midnight".

Is that why I'm "protected" by disruptions? Is that why i'm able to hijack my immunity and stop my skin from flaring? Is that why 6 hours of sleep from before 9pm = complete rejuvenation?

Something else I have noticed too:

When I wake up @ 3am, if I start thinking too much and getting even the tiniest bit stressed - I start to feel an itch on my skin. Today I simply just deep breath in and out and relaxed my whole body and the itch completely disappeared.

Could this be, Cortisol raised at the WRONG Circadian time = Auto-immunity/ Eczema at that time?

Could Cortisol being raised, way before it's rhythmic time @ around sunset, cause immune cells to become over-active, or dysfunctional?

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-17T16:25:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248683>

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

Hahaha "miraculously" yeah. I have cut those out with my girlfriend too, or anyone in fact.

I have a rule now - no "activity" beyond 7pm.

That includes, eating (finish at 4 most days), being on the phone or computer, TV, cooking, tidying, washing, exercise, and even "discussions" with the girlfriend.

They all raise my Cortisol and delay me from sleeping. So, I have switched all my "stressful" activities to first thing in the morning when Cortisol is High anyway - and now I get such a good sleep - and my body has fell into this pattern really really well.

I can cook, wash up, get ready for work, do some revision or reading, check social media and do all my "thinking" before 7am everyday.

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-17T17:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248687>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East\_India\_Company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_India_Company)

You probably already know of all this but I have just been reading that page for the last 2 hours. It's actually a time when the British and Europeans would have started to become exposed to the likes of turmeric, ginger, nutmeg, cinnamon and all the textiles and cottons and teas. And Deuterium in Foods, available in Winter!

So the EIC was basically a Trading company in South East Asia, turned Army (after being given the rights by King Charles 1 to basically do what they fuck they want on behalf of the Crown) for the British Empire. It was eventually just absorbed into the Empire and became the British Raj or Crown of India, and ruled it until independence about 100 years ago.

Think of all these new foods arriving in old England... and the fact people used to avoid being outdoors as it was considered "poor". The UK and the Dutch started breaking Jack's seasonal eating rules A LONG TIME AGO!

Why do I get the features of a cannabis high, and not another drug or just endorphins?

I've googled everywhere for other people who have experienced it. Most on the internet are on "weed" forums haha... they are full of "duuuuuuuude" and "sweeeeeeet" comments everywhere you look. And absolutely zero science. People just saying "nah bullshit, impossible" etc. Others have said, "maybe its the THC stored in your brain cells".

So, I checked that hypothesis and pretty much every source says that yes "burning fat" i.e. by exercise or weight loss (from a Leptin Reset perhaps - and I have lost weight!) can destroy Fat Cells and therefore release concentrations of THC into the blood. But that it would only increase from 1% to 1.25%, and therefore you wouldn't feel it.... HENCE THE FACT THAT MILLION OF PEOPLE SMOKE WEED, LOSE WEIGHT, AND DONT GET THIS EXPERIENCE.

So, that is that theory put to bed. Plus, I haven't smoked weed in years so why would this not have happened when I first went Keto in 2016, or another time since those days?

Also, if that was the case - why would Omega 3 be a factor in THC release from fat cells?

Perhaps, DHA improves the receptors on my Brain Cells that have Cannabinoid Receptors (CB1). And therefore, any THC released is activating the receptors as they are SO sensitive.

But that would also suggest I have some unbelievable brain cell power above others, and based on the fact I'm still not 100% recovered - albeit nearly there - but I'm no WIM HOF... So that just doesn't make sense.

All I know is that i'm tapering the affect a little bit with eating more Meats like Beef and Pork to blunt the effect, as to be honest it can be a bit overwhelming, especially at work, I can't be HIGH at work lol!!

At the weekend though, it is pretty amazing - this Saturday I had it all day and felt fucking incredibly sociable, happy, aware and also - got the giggles of course - so I'm laughing and joking with everyone. Finding everything hilarious... Which is absolutely amazing, I've tapped into a Free Natural Recreational Drug Dealer in my brain my eating Fish...... Who the fuck can say they have done that?

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-21T17:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248993>

**Skin, Libido, Energy, Sleep:
**
Continued to have better skin corresponding to better Sleep. The nights where I've been asleep by 9pm I've continued to wake up without scratching, except for 1 day at the weekend where I had Squid for Dinner. It was the only change, so it was isolated, and I've had similar night reactions to Oysters and Shellfish. So the proteins must still be causing an immune/ stress response. Although, I get no other symptoms, not even a bad gut, just several wake ups and intense scratching. Maybe this is due to HISTAMINE being present in Seafood in high amounts, and nothing to do with a leaky gut/ immune response. Either way, Shellfish is off the menu - along with carbs, spices, fruits - for dinnertime. I'm still eating Oysters for Breakfast, which doesn't affect my skin on the following night's sleep.

I have a picture of my hands eczema from July when they were bleeding and inflamed to hell. I should take one now so you can see the difference. Certain parts of the day it looks invisible, usually Early in AM when blood pressure is low. Afternoon time it all swells and puffs, you can see the damaged tissues deep beneath the skin, this is due to blood pressure rising in afternoon. Who can tell I've reading a bit on circadian biology?

I have taken it upon myself to start EATING MORE. Just more protein and fat. The light levels are starting to drop in the UK now. And Saturday/ Sunday we had grey clouds all day and no Sun. Man I got SO HUNGRY! No Sun to activate POMC and also low dopamine. Instantly I'm craving more food, but not Carbs - which is good. I want big protein heavy fatty meals, like a slab of Meat and Butter. A cheese burger the size of my head is what I really want. Skip the cheese if need be - just want that meat.

My thoughts: maybe as light levels wane at the end of Summer, all mammals have reduced dopamine which encourages hunger and cravings at the end of summer. So, foraging increases and weight is gained.

[http://www.hallobay.com/about-hallo-bay/seasonal-bear-activity/](http://www.hallobay.com/about-hallo-bay/seasonal-bear-activity/)

Just have a look at the link above about Alaskan Bears and their seasonal eating and habits. To quote:

_**"Mid-August to Mid-September**
The chum and pink salmon spawning runs are completing and the Coho salmon run is just beginning. The Coho salmon is the largest run of salmon in our coastal area with the run continuing into October. The Coho salmon averages much larger in weight than the pink and chum and usually has more fat, which the bears seems to prefer. In late August the bears are starting to show their beginning coats of new winter fur and as the daysget shorter in length, the fur seems to grow even faster. **There also seems to now be urgency for the bears to eat as much as possible, perhaps in anticipation of the coming winter. The bears are now supplementing their fish diet with a large variety of mushrooms and wild berries, which are ripening.** Excellent bear viewing."_

So this assumed Urgency for Food in prep for WInter, how would that happen physiologically. Well Less Light via the Eye = low dopamine = food cravings.

Cravings are actually built into us to provide a kick up the arse to get more food for WInter.

So, on that basis - I've doubled up my evening dinners - I've added 250g Beef Mince and 100g of Pork Scratchings (Roasted Pork Fat) to my 450g Wild Salmon, and another few tbsp of Coconut Oil to my 8 tbsp already. It is a MONSTER MEAL!

Since doing so, I've noticed an instant response in my body mass. My Ghrelin has increased, I'm getting hungrier now my body has spotted access to the food - how? I don't know. I'm not hungry for Carbs. If I was - i'd probably get back on the Leptin Reset. I'm just craving slabs of meat and fat!

I've also noticed a **Hot Flush and Fatigue and a bit of a Crash** after that big meal. It's not like it was a few months ago. But it shows i'm still a bit Insulin Resistant.... but I am going really Hot after the big ass dinner - i'm radiating heat and sweating - which I have read is a sign of Uncoupling Protein 1 & 3 activation - which is the "burning excess cals as heat" and is a good thing!

I'm going to monitor the above, I'm all for eating more and gaining some weight, but I also don't want any fatigue, ever again.

Lastly, I got to sleep @ 8pm last night and I woke @ midnight, it is usually 2 or 3pm, and then asleep til 5am! I got that REM sleep from 3-5am in and not disrupted - the result? MAJOR LIBIDO!

So NREM seems to be the factor in skin and immunity, and REM seems to be the factor my testosterone, libido and growth.

In fact, as Matt Walker describes in his book - **NREM is like the chisel or the pick axe on the sculpture, clearing away all the debris and unnecessary clay (immunity). REM on the otherhand is the creativity (dreams, thoughts, sexual creativity), the addition of details and colour - the growth of the sculpture (testosterone).**

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-23T16:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-249191>

Decided to go on a hunt for some academic research to give some understanding to my theories and thoughts.

With a high proportion of NREM (slow wave sleep) occuring EARLY in the night, and a high proportion of REM sleep occurring in the LATER hours of the night, it could be said that someone who sleeps from 9pm - 5am would have MORE NREM SLEEP than someone who sleeps from 11pm - 7am.

Matthew Walker actually confirms the above statement here in this article:
****[http://www.pbs.org/wgbh/nova/body/walker-sleep.html](http://www.pbs.org/wgbh/nova/body/walker-sleep.html)****
So my early bedtimes of "pre-9pm" or Geriatric as we say (I'm asleep by 8.15pm now!!), mean that I get a whole lot more NREM than my former self aged 18 - who got to bed @ 1am most nights, and had heavy lucid dreams that used to leave me comatosed for the morning and missing college lessons, which confirms the REM Sleep I was getting.

However, at 18 I was in full swing with symptoms and problems... Crohn's/IBD and Eczema and Depression. So maybe REM is not so good for that. So @ 25 i have this theory I need to go to bed earlier - and it has worked wonders - so what is it about the early bedtime. It MUST link with NREM Sleep. So i found the below article:
**
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3256323/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3256323/)**

**Sleep and immune function, extract:**
"Moreover, such studies revealed a selectively enhancing influence of sleep on cytokines promoting the interaction between antigen presenting cells and T helper cells, like interleukin-12. Sleep on the night after experimental vaccinations against hepatitis A produced a strong and persistent increase in the number of antigen-specific Th cells and antibody titres. T**ogether these findings indicate a specific role of sleep in the formation of immunological memory. This role appears to be associated in particular with the stage of slow wave sleep (NREM Sleep) and the accompanying pro-inflammatory endocrine milieu that is hallmarked by high growth hormone and prolactin levels and low cortisol and catecholamine concentrations.**"

Immunological Memory.

That is exactly, what my immune system, DOESN'T HAVE aged 18. My immune system was, and still is, attacking my own Cells. That is NOT good immunological memory.

This same NREM period has increased Growth Hormone (protective of epithelials, like the skin) and lowered Cortisol (which I have already established a link with to my Eczema).

So I think it can be said, based on the above:

**Increasing NREM Sleep by being asleep early i.e. 8pm - 9pm, is an effective support therapy for the recovery of immunological memory dysfunction disorders, such as Auto-immunity (Crohn's, Rheumathoid, Eczema, Hashimoto's etc.)**

### Sean Waters — 2018-09-05T19:36:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-250264>

Couple weeks off and I've got some more updates:

1\. I added Meat, Grass Fed Beef and Outdoor bred Pork to my dietary template which was previously ALL Fish. This did not end well, I developed an episode of Depression after about 1 week of eating it. All of those "stoned" highs and endorphins I was getting disappeared. This was damn good Meat too - but unfortunately, my body doesn't like it. Back to eating Fish and it disappears.

2\. I've actually had to cut out Oysters altogether. I know this is an instant "WTF" moment from everyone reading. But, honestly, they were too much for my Leaky Gut - says a lot about my redox - and I feel a lot better without them. I don't rely on them for my Libido or Clarity either, I get on absolutely great without them. [@Michalis](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18092/) has experienced similar issues. Maybe it is the source of our Oysters. They are wild and renowned good quality, but could be wrong! Either way, I'm doing great without them.

3\. All spices and any inflammatory foods are out the window. Not even Nutmeg or Oregano are allowed in. If I stick to pure meals such as Fish, Salt and Ghee/coconut oil I fair much much better.

4\. My breakfast works an absolute treat: 4 eggs, 20g of Sea Greens, 100g Ghee (5 or 6 tbsp when melted), 1 onion and Turmeric 1 tbsp.
This seems to be the perfect formula for a good day. If I vary, I get in trouble!

5\. I've added 3000mg or 3g of Curcumin Capsules that I used 3 years ago to engineer a Crohn's Disease reversal (to this day I've not had this back - even through my recent Fatigue/Eczema issues). The brand I use is the same one. Good Health Naturally Curcumin X4000 if anyone is interested.

The reason i've started Curcumin is because of listening to the Nov 2017 Deuterium Webinar. Jack said that Resveratrol and Curcumin increase the voltage or charge on the inner mitochondrial membrane and deplete deuterium. Back in his EPCOTx protocol he recommended 3-5g per day. That's about £100-150 per month. I'm doing just that, and back in 2015 for the crohn's I also used 3g per day to get a reversal. The dose really is the key here.

The other reason I started was because I had a theory, that if my Inflammation is Low my Cortisol will be Low, and my NREM sleep will improve - and therefore my immunity and my libido and redox will all improve.

Exactly that has happened.

I've become that confident in my redox i've began exercising regularly for the first time in over 18 months. No Fatigue, no problems. UNLESS, it is after 5pm, then I ruin my sleep. But beforehand seems fine. I also get endorphins afterwards, and not a stress feeling or headache. Barefoot sprints in the Sunshine at the park are my go to now.

My Eczema has subsided so much that I don't actually NEED that pre-9pm bed anymore to stop a flare up. I just don't get them. This is so so key. But I still get my pre-9pm bedtime as I find there is more than just the histamine benefits.

I really can't recommend the Curcumin at 2-3g per day any more! It is has helped me two times to reverse disease!!

6\. I've found a new house to move into! It just round the corner and my life will be unaffected. But it is MOLD FREE and also allows me my own space away from my partner who I'm becoming distant from at the moment. My determination to succeed with my health has pushed us right apart.... we'll see what happens on that one.

I still can't sleep fully through the night in this current house. But I can at my parents. The difference, MOLD I think! We will see when I move out.....

7\. I'm eyeing the Winter that is approaching - and I'm excited!!!

The CT is going to be unbelievable. I've found since my inflamm has lowered that my WARMTH has increased. I feel naturally ready to do CT, I don't feel like I will be forcing it or going against my will. Which I think is important.

Summary

\- I'm really focused at work and putting in a real good performance. Cognitively I'm really fast and I feel i'm actually getting ahead of my colleagues. Remember I work all day in a nnEMF blue lit hell hole office - and I'm getting better... important to mention for those reading who feel they need to get up and quit everything. LOOK DEEPER!!

\- Eczema so close to being gone - just feel it's a case of getting out of this mold house in the next 2 weeks and then it will disappear.

\- Looking forward to CT as the water temps will drop in about 4 weeks to a nice level for free CT in my bathtub (MOLD FREE BATHTUB TOO  ) I'm hoping to go at it like WIM HOF and be adapted by Xmas and really take off and put these symptoms behind me.

The target is to be so "well" and such good Redox that I can take Eating out or drinking or socialising.... and get my life back to how I want it!

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

I think that is it for now, PEACE

Hey Sean - Great journal. Each time you posted something about this, it reminded me of something I once read about using IR sauna to detox from heroin. If I recall correctly, the idea was that patients did sauna while using naltrexone. The IR helped detox the drugs from the body over time. Without naltrexone, patients would have the same experience you are having, and would relapse.

Perhaps your body is releasing old stuff in large enough quantities for it to effect you. Or, with your idea of increased sensitivity, perhaps it's enough. Either way, it's another place to do some research.

Cheers!

### Elizabeth G — 2018-09-06T12:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-250345>

In my mind, epigenetics are always in play! That being said, if you clean up the environment the switches can turn on/off. You’ve already done a lot of that.

I saw this article about O3 generating endocannabinoids. [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2017/07/170718142909.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2017/07/170718142909.htm)

The endos have higher affinity for the anti-inflammatory receptors. Let’s assume for a moment that your fat cells are releasing THC as they die. Let’s also assume you’re still around you Dad or some friends who may be smoking. (studies show that while the amount of THC in second-hand smoke tends to be barely perceptible in most, it is perceptible in some). THC can bind to neuro- and anti-inflammatory sites.

You have this old THC and possibly some new second-hand THC, and increased sensitivity. Increasing endocannabinoids via O3, bumps the THC out of the anti-inflammatory site, this increasing circulating THC, where it is now available for a neuro site.

Perhaps your environment has caused upregulation of receptors in the brain. No time to look for a study to see if this could be true.

Now, all that being said, the root cause could be in a system “upstream” of the cannabinol dream raptors. (Ha! An autocorrect that I have to leave -cannabinoid receptors) I don’t know what is, but here’s my related example:

I was having reactions to sulfur. If I ate garlic and onions, I could smell it so strongly on my palms that it would wake me up st night. If I helped clear the sulfa pathway with molybdenum, I could eat those things and not experience the odor. There were other reactions too, but we’ll keep it simple for the story. Since I love onions and garlic, I started taking the molybdenum regularly and happily.

Separately, I unintentionally flushed out a bunch of yeast from my GI tract. Suddenly, the above sulfur problem resolved. Why?

The acetylaldehyde, a by-product in the yeast metabolism, was using up all of my molybdenum and there wasn’t any left for garlic and onions! Molybdenum was a patch, but whatever was stealing my molybdenum was the root cause. Could there be a steal going on somewhere that is hindering your ability to get the THC out?

Ok. I’m late to the morning duties. Gotta go!

### Sean Waters — 2018-09-10T20:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-250660>

Well I've definitely not been doing things right today...........

It's a big week at work, once a month we deal with payments, and the whole place gets tense and chaotic. I've been working hard to get ahead and I thought I was doing good until my Boss started pointing out everything I haven't done.

It was never clear to me he wanted all that stuff until a couple days ago. Frankly, I'm the 7th or 8th person in my chair in the last year. He's notoriously difficult as a Boss and so many people have quit this place lately, it's a really bad job.

And I was doing fine until this meeting - but last night I had so many disruptions to my sleep - I had a bit of an allergic reaction to something and there was a party going on downstairs. Me and my girlfriend are stressed too as we are borderline splitting up but sharing a room. It's been a very difficult few weeks.

anyway, I just erupted in this meeting after 10 minutes of being derided. I asked him "what's your Fucking problem?" and I was close to going for him. I should have known better, I froze afterwards and realised what I'd done, and then I just couldn't speak. It was a real volcano moment. And I just had to breathe for about 10 mins before I could speak again.

He took it well, and could see I was stressed and asked me about my health etc. I know he knows he pushed me too far. So I think we are ok, but I'm in a awkward position - should I leave the business? Should I stay? I don't really know where I stand.

But from a health perspective, it has left me confused. Thought I was doing really well... maybe I am and it was just a blemish?

Bit confused by it to be honest.

And then I re-read a line I'd sent to Michaelis this morning when helping him:

"sometimes mistakes are what we need. They make us learn better"

I am a firm believer of "Win, or Learn". I've erupted like that quite a few times before, but not in a work scenario. I feel I've let myself down. But, I don't want to quit - I've got something to prove to myself and to my Boss by making a good comeback and bouncing back.... the weak person inside me is saying just quit, but something is saying no.

### Michalis — 2018-09-10T21:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-250671>

Erupting in workplace : Objectively - it can hurt you professionally, however you can be 100% right in your feelings about him or the job. Have i done it? Yes! Was it worth the hassle? I have found myself better off couple of times. (I AM **NOT** SAYING QUIT). Have i regretted it? I had and only because i made myself look like a tit.

Having said that, it doesn't mean that you have to erupt in the workplace, you will learn from that mistake now because you have seen the other side. Since you know your are ahead of the curve already so don't replicate it at home with the missus if you have escalations. Even if you got the best dopamine in the world you can have these thoughts and feelings.. I think having substantial dopamine helps our quantum computer to work right but that is not the whole story. The rest is culture and temperament and wisdom. And others!

It takes two to tango and in my opinion some bosses are taking the piss too much. Those who know to how to lead inspire people to work better, they don't deride or micromanage others. If your boss was smart he would not have all this friction culture in the office. I think he has been arousing your indignation and that seems to be a business as usual situation over there , as apparently there is staff turnover in that position. Ironically, coercion was a think of the past, now there are more subtle , human ways to make you work properly... Maybe he needs to go get an MBA, Maybe this guy likes to push peoples' buttons. Maybe he is stressed too because his boss is doing the same to him. Maybe he is trying to stay afloat. Maybe his wife is not giving it to him any more. But in the end of the day it doesn't really matter as you are probably not hanging out after work or you are not on the same wavelength.

You usually learn to live with these kind of situations, especially if you got mouths to feed and mortgages and the whole shebang. The reality is stress + cortisol spikes and not switching off when you go home. Eventually you can be burnt off. It can undo what you do at home. It can affect your relationship and vice versa. Then the big picture arises .. All these trivial things can add up and raise a tsunami.

The good thing is that you are a contractor like me. The world (or London for now) is our oyster and we can jump from company to company easily. Personally when they grind my gears i am out, i am not playing in their matrix anymore and it is liberating. But then i need to deal with it. But that doesn't mean you need to do what i do.
I had amazing contracts and bosses in the past which i really loved and never wanted to end. Seriously. And i had wankers too.. I don't know what your industry is like and if there are a lot of jobs going on, those things you know better obviously to make a decision how to proceed. If you decide to leave at some point in the future get your best behavior on for a while , put up with him while looking for something else. That might provoke him to push your buttons harder. But you know better after this.

What i personally believe is that we are like drones ... Corporate fucking drones .. If you play the game right you go up or stay afloat.. The crazy thing about the whole thing is that your indignation **CAN be** healthy but it is unhealthy for the business that you work at. This is completely incongruent and it is causing people a lot of problems...

### Sean Waters — 2018-09-28T11:41:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-251872>

So Jack posts this link:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22959887](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22959887)

Docosahexaenoylethanolamide, the structural analog of the endogenous cannabinoid receptor ligand anandamide, is synthesized from DHA in the brain. Although docosahexaenoylethanolamide binds weakly to cannabinoid receptors, it stimulates neurite growth, synaptogenesis and glutamatergic synaptic activity in developing hippocampal neurons at concentrations of 10-100 nM. We have previously proposed the term synaptamide for docosahexaenoylethanolamide **_to emphasize its potent synaptogenic activity and structural similarity to anandamide._** Synaptamide is subjected to hydrolysis by fatty acid amide hydrolase, and can be oxygenated to bioactive metabolites. The brain synaptamide content is dependent on the dietary DHA intake, suggesting an endogenous mechanism whereby diets containing adequate amounts of omega-3 fatty acids improve synaptogenesis in addition to well-recognized anti-inflammatory effects.

**Anandamide = Sanskrit word for “joy, bliss, or happiness.”

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21810478](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21810478)
**
Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), the n-3 essential fatty acid that is highly enriched in the brain, increases neurite growth and synaptogenesis in cultured mouse fetal hippocampal neurons. **_These cellular effects may underlie the DHA-induced enhancement of hippocampus-dependent learning and memory functions. We found that N-docsahexaenoylethanolamide (DEA), an ethanolamide derivative of DHA, is a potent mediator for these actions._**

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23570577](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23570577)

DHA has been shown to **_promote neuronal differentiation of neural stem cells (NSCs) in vivo and in vitro._** Previously, we found that N-docosahexaenoylethanolamine (synaptamide), an endogenous DHA metabolite with an endocannabinoid-like structure, promotes neurite growth, synaptogenesis, and synaptic function. In this study, we demonstrate that synaptamide potently induces neuronal differentiation of NSCs. Differentiating NSCs were capable of synthesizing synaptamide from DHA. Treatment of NSCs with synaptamide at low nanomolar concentrations significantly increased the number of MAP2 and Tuj-1-positive neurons with concomitant induction of protein kinase A (PKA)/cAMP response element binding protein (CREB) phosphorylation. Conversely, PKA inhibitors or PKA knockdown abolished the synaptamide-induced neuronal differentiation of NSCs. URB597, a fatty acid amide hydrolase (FAAH) inhibitor, elevated the level of DHA-derived synaptamide and further potentiated the DHA- or synaptamide-induced neuronal differentiation of NSCs. Similarly, NSCs obtained from FAAH KO mice exhibited greater capacity to induce neuronal differentiation in response to DHA or synaptamide compared to the wild type NSCs. Neither synaptamide nor DHA affected NSC differentiation into GFAP-positive glia cells. These results suggest that endogenously produced synaptamide is a potent mediator for neurogenic differentiation of NSCs acting through PKA/CREB activation.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23088259](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23088259)

it has already been shown some time ago that consumption of n-3 LC-PUFAs not only affects the synthesis of prototypic endocannabinoids like anandamide **_but also stimulates the formation of specific n-3 LC-PUFA-derived conjugates with ethanolamine, dopamine, serotonin or other amines._** Some of these fatty amides show overlapping biological activities with those of typical endocannabinoids, whereas others possess distinct and sometimes largely unknown receptor affinities and other properties. The ethanolamine and dopamine conjugates of DHA have been the most investigated thus far.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Can't really state the benefits of 03/dha much more can you.

but it seems to be heavily involved in hippocampus neural function, and this means memory.... so may be why im experiencing these flashbacks

### Sean Waters — 2018-09-28T16:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-251887>

SO i've moved into my new house.... first few days were fantastic. The Mold Free environment is great and the reduction in stress and the new environment has meant I've had some really good surges in dopamine and general wellness. It is far less stressful, and I feel much much better operating from that place.

However, the landlord provided me with an old and battered mattress, and by the 3rd day of sleeping on this on the floor i started having eczema flares, but NOT in my usual spots, in and around my midriff, lower back and groin area (let's say lol). I also have had severe tinnitus when i lie down too... well that points to magnetic fields, and im thinking the WiFi located in the kitchen is jump conducting onto the pipes above it (as the central heating system runs above it) and is coming into my room and under the floorboards. And as i am sleeping 15cm off the floor in an old battered mattress it could be an issue. On further review, there is nothing between me and the springs but a few layers of cotton as it so battered - literally no cushion. So, im right in line for this EMF antenna mattress. So i wake up and im exhausted and scratched the living daylights out of myself, and i have this idea at 2am that it is the mattress. So i start putting layers of fabric, clothes and the quilt between me and the mattress. I also fold my pillows so my head is raised away from it. What happens? The tinnitus and eczema fade......

So, i get up yesterday and barely make it to work. I spend 3 hours finding a new mattress for delivery that night. I get one, a memory foam with no springs or metal. Only £170 inc. delivery at 6pm that night.

It arrives, and the guy looks at the old one and is mortified. He said, you probably got "bed bugs" as he has NO IDEA about EMF's. But he notes there is nothing between me and the springs. He suggests complaining to landlord. Which i did today. Anyway....

I make up my bed and black out the room as usual and fall off at around 8.30pm as usual (my melatonin and circadian sensitivity to darkness is so acute and responsive now - one thing i'm proud of. I drop off in this house so easily, despite such loud drumming in my ears from tinnitus.

I wake up about 5 times through the night, as i can smell the chemical off-gassing from the foam and i have more allergic reactions. This time, back on my hands and fingers (like i used to get with Mold and Bleach flare ups), but zero around groin and midriff. But im also REALLY WARM from the foam.

So i wake up to ANOTHER dreadful night's sleep and i can barely get up. I'm late for work as i literally couldn't get out bed til 7am, usually im out at 5 and getting in ice baths!

I'm hoping by airing out my foam mattress today will remove some chemicals and that i wont react as bad and will begin to sleep ok.

I'm fearing the worst though. As the EMF in this house is fucking REAL. As the tinnitus is so loud when im in the house.

There is an intensive care nurse living upstairs who works nightshifts, but her boyfriend who isn't supposed to be there and is using the house to "chill in" for a while as he finds a job. He's a biomedical science degree dude from netherlands. And he thinks my blue light avoidance is crazy. And thinks melatonin suppression at night is fine, because it will just be released later and it won't make a difference...... i left him Russell Foster's book Rhythms of Life and said, "you think so? Read that and tell me you still think phase-shifting your circadian rhythm is wise." Anyway, he just skimmed it and said "interesting" and has avoided talking about it. Oh the joy of keeping your walls and beliefs upheld in your ignorant modern primate brain.

Anyway, I am bouncing back a lot better these days, and a bad sleep is devastation in the morning, but by mid morning i'm alright. So there are good things happening.

But, I start college tomorrow for my nutritional therapy/functional med career to get back on track. Which i'm dubious about even doing because it's so fucked up as we know, but it's the only route available to make a career out of this.

I fear I may be taking too much on. I have 5 weekends off in the next 12 weeks. Plus full time work 8 til 5 everyday. I only have 2 days off in the next 4 weeks. So I'm going to need everything to be stable behind me as I work through this... but something says that's a fantasy.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

I feel as if my trials and obstacles that i've faced over the years are always to teach me something. I learn best when I suffer. And all the bullshit i've been through this year has led me to be way smarter about Leptin and metabolism, and circadian rhythms, and the skin and the brain - to the point i could help others with that knowledge, and not just refer them to ABC Diet / Paleo bullshit. I actually know from experience, how these things work....... that is valuable to a patient in my clinic, right? and maybe that is why i go through these trials, and shouldn't give in?

Let's face it. Jack didn't go through this easily, and he faced real life issues that taught him more than any textbook. He's also so knowledgeable from his self-experiments, which enabled a lot of predictions with no data - which is why paleo thought him fucking crazy. But look at it now, tables have turned!

Whether you believe in higher powers or not, I feel like I am going through these trials to become wiser and better from it. Whether it's just probability or whether it's a magical deity being, this has happened - i have trials, and i have learnt - and im better and smarter all the same. But, days like today i wonder if its actually telling me to get the fuck out of London - and stop bothering. And that i'd be better off just getting back to my parents in the country and going back to work up there for less money. I can switch colleges and continue the course. I do feel a lot better there. And importantly, reverse a disease there before.

I don't know why but my gut is saying no, and there is a reason im in london that i can't see yet.

It's all a bit superstitious, and this may be a low dopamine mind struggling to get out of his cage. Bit like your biomed dutch guy with the walls and beliefs. Maybe im conditioned to not let go of the chains around me......... Like [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) told me, the story about the elephants and the rope.... the adult elephants can break the rope that binds them as slaves, yet they are conditioned to believe they can't as babies, so they never escape the weak ties even as adults. Is that happening here? Do I need to just get the fuck out?

Well i tried that and ran away to mexico and put my arse there and said ok motherfuckers - show me the way - only to be bounced right back to the UK to learn how it all actually ties together and that running away wasn't the answer.

IT's not as bad as January, I really was fucked at that point, desperately fucked. Now i'm doing really well (once i find a suitable mattress), i work and earn money and im slowly improving.

Guess this is more of a decision about sticking this out for a year before i got money to travel for a year or so, or just getting out now and not putting myself through this shit anymore.

### Sean Waters — 2018-10-02T11:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-252152>

Another night of CT to block the scratching from this Mattress...... Or, is it not the mattress but I have hit the ceiling with my reversals in London?

Again, the CT felt good but I still scratched like a maniac, and it was a body part that I really don't want to be sore... i'll leave that there.

So because of this, I had a moment of clarity while my body was stinging and reeling from the eczema damage @ 3am this morning.

**"Why the fuck am I here?"**

I've already proved enough. I have reversed Chronic Fatigue and Depression in London, the EMF hell hole of the world. That is excellent.

But, I'm still skinny as fuck. I still can't sleep. And my whole body is still suffering with Eczema.......

I think I have hit the ceiling with what I can achieve here. Yes, this new mattress arriving tomorrow may reduce the scratching, but I don't think it will solve the Eczema.

I've had it since I was born. I came out with a rash. It was the first thing to appear and say: "your melanopsin is screwed" at the age of 5 minutes old in the hospital on that Friday night.

I've managed to get round it in recent years, and I have actually corrected my circadian rhythm so well that I've beat Adrenal Fatigue and Mental Issues AND Crohn's disease is still nowhere to be seen.

But, with all the EMF hitting me in this city and my house, which we know now can disrupt Vitamin A from Melanopsin and destroy Melatonin all by itself, I don't think I am ever going to sleep through the night here. I have only done it once in 6 months, at my parents house.

Despite all the scientific weighing up of the above, the fact is - WHY AM I TOLERATING SOMETHING THAT IS NOT GOOD ENOUGH FOR ME?!!!!

Even if this was 10% suboptimal (it's more like 80%), WHY STAND FOR IT?

AM I NOT GOOD ENOUGH FOR MYSELF?

I finally woke up today. And I won't be tolerating this shit anymore. I'm heading back to my parents in a month. Switching college to the one near my home. And getting a job locally, for probably the same if not more money. I'll be with my friends. Rent Free.

And most importantly, I'll able to get optimal once again. If i don't, well then I'll go somewhere else - I won't stop now until I get what I deserve.

I'll be handing my notice in the next few days. Goodbye London, Hello countryside home.

### Sean Waters — 2018-10-08T11:14:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-252610>

BIG UPDATE.......

Turns out I haven't got Eczema at all.

I've actually caught a parasite from the house I was in before, and after applying some anti-parasitic powder and oils to my skin, it has cleared up everywhere except a few severe spots: my hands, crotch, lower back and back of knees. But even those spots now look and feel normal. It will all be gone within a couple of months of treatment.

I can't actually believe it was this all along. And I felt really stupid initially, for not seeing it, but what made it difficult to spot is the noone else in the house, including my girlfriend in the same bed as me, have anything like this. So Why Did I Get It? Redox I guess. And according to my Mom, who actually suggested that it might not be eczema, I had a similar exposure when I was a child which makes you really susceptible to it later on. But there is no doubt in my mind that having a SKIN PARASITE is really not a good thing...... if my Skin which is the biggest UV surface receiver in my body is being opportunistically taken advantage of by a Parasite, it has to mean I am lacking UV Light, or COLD...

But it also explains why in my lab results back in July that all my T3 and Leptin panels were all in order EXCEPT my HS-CRP... why? Cause I was fighting a parasite. Now that I've started getting rid of it my Libido is back and my dreams are incredibly vivid - which makes me think my Redox is coming back. It also explains why I'm so skinny, all my energy is used to fight this thing off. Hopefully now things will return to normal.......

Anyway, I've had to literally Freeze all my clothes and hot wash all my whites. I've bought a new mattress and new clothes too. It has been a fucking stress. And I'm still having very mild flares in the night in the severe spots, but about 80% less intense and I actually wake up feeling refreshed now (most days).

It's frustrating trying to get rid of it, as I'm having to constantly recycle and freeze and wash my clothes - which has been difficult with not having a day off work in 15 days (will be 20 by Friday), and Work/College + Eat + Sleep + Repeat. Today I was so exhausted when waking up that I had no time to prepare and just haven't got any food today and I'm just fasting. I haven't even got clean shirts for work LOL. Jesus I look an absolute mess hahaha

BUT ON THE PLUS SIDE, a huge positive is: At some point in the next few months THIS WILL ALL BE OVER

I can finally start getting my life back in order. I'm not going to leave London just yet, as I'd have to completely change my life right in the middle of semester and find a new job etc. So I'm gonna stick it out til next July when I finish my 2nd year of college and I will have substantial savings.

Then I'm thinking of travelling around India and Asia, for 6-9 months, and build my redox.... Fatigue free, Eczema/ skin rash/ parasite free, crohn's free...

It's been a crazy 10 months since starting this Journal:
I've been to India and back, Mexico and back, started a Leptin Reset, reversed my fatigue, made some huge findings in my brain regarding Omega 3, made huge gains in my circadian rhythm understanding AND practical application, and now I'm starting Clinical Practice Training at College I feel so much more knowledgeable and USEFUL to a potential Client...... This whole process has made me suffer and at points I wanted to just run away again, but in the end I am now 10x the person I was in January, and I have so much knowledge about all the things above that I can really genuinely help people with similar problems.

Well I actually manage the Water company mentioned in the article at work on the 2nd biggest residential project in the UK, you'd think they would try but they don't - they are useless. The telecom companies are useless too. I'm not expecting this to be done or finished anytime soon.

However, the clock is ticking...

My parents house out in the country is in a village that still to this day doesn't receive a 3G or 4G signal, you can only get GPRS or the "E" signal. It's actually only 5-10 min drive from where you can get it, but it is near one of the country's oldest canal systems and it is all preserved and Green Belt land - and it is precious to the locals and the county - they are still furious that the Motorway was built so close 20 years ago...... based on this, i'm hopeful that I can always go back to my parents and be protected from 5G for a good few years, and that the locals will keep blocking any "new things" or "ugly eye saw cell towers" from being constructed. We actually still have powercuts from time to time, the whole village just drops out. There is also the option of getting on the Government's Affordable Housing Scheme, as I have been registered to live there all my life - I would be able to buy a house there for half the price, while the government subsidizes the other half of the mortgage. I'm definitely considering this as a long term option.

However, I am also considering living abroad, as low EMF village is ok - the UV is fucking terrible. So I think I'm going to head to the Far East and try set up there for a few years. Or maybe split my time between Home and South East Asia, 6 months each.

They are building all over Thailand, Cambodia, Indonesia, Malaysia etc. and there is 20-30 years worth of Hotels, Homes and Infrastructure to be built. Plus, they are quite lenient on Visas and they want Western Professionals to come and bring their expertise. The company I work for now is Singapore based, so I may try get in with them somehow.

Not sure how I'd be able to start Nutritional Therapy/ Functional Med in South East Asia, but I know there are Func. Med conferences in Bangkok and Malaysia all the time - so maybe I could? Who knows.... I'd be happy working construction in though, and maybe doing NT/FM back in UK.

Cambodia is 10.6253° N (Sihanoukville/ Koh Rong). It is also really fucking cheap and super friendly. So I'm thinking maybe going and working over there in the next couple years, earning my £ and then seeing where I'm at.

It's hard to plan when life changes so rapidly for me all the time. All I can do is start looking ahead and creating ideas for how I could live at the Equator and EARN money. I'm 25 and i've already been hit like 3 times with diseases, how long am I able to keep reversing them? Will their come a time where a Leptin Reset stops working... Based on Jack's move to the Farm, I think the answer is 100% yes, and we aren't even 5G yet. Can we go 6 and 7G in the future too? What will that bring to my fucked up Mitochondria?

The Central Americas aren't possible, you can't work and earn money there, unless it's your own business - and even then laws are kind of weird about you actually "labouring". I think South America is the same. Luckily, Asia has some high UV location work for me.

So at 25 years old I have a long time to navigate through this world, and I need £ unless I go nomadic (which I have considered). I've also started to consider what I'm gonna do in 5-10 years when I want to have a family. There is much to think about. The World is changing fast........

### Sean Waters — 2018-10-15T12:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-253226>

Update.......

So I'm on a daily routine of killing off these mites/parasites, it is working but some of these fuckers are deep-rooted and it is taking some time. Apparently, it is up to 3 months to clear it all. It is a constant battle, covering my bed in this Diatomaceous Earth powder and then constantly repleneshing sheets and clothes by Hot Washing or even Freezing them. Anyway, that is ongoing and my skin is WAY better and so is my sleep.

**Sauna**
I've also started using Finnish Saunas, roughly 80-100°C (176-212°F). I'm going pretty much everyday after work and for about 15-20 mins at a time, followed by a Cold Shower for 10-15 mins.

I know the Heat Shock Proteins are part of the quilt and mentioned in the EMF Rx webinar for Ornithine Decarboxylase being protective of EMF in the body. But I've not come across any blogs that detail Saunas and their benefits by Jack...

I thought by sweating out these mites that might be a good idea to use the Sauna. I've also found some other benefits in the last week:

\- Major increases in Libido, especially Post-Sauna, Pre-Sleep and upon Waking
\- Massive increases in Body Heat, especially upon waking/ during sleep. I feel like a furnace!
\- Body composition improvements, even in just 1 week I feel a difference
\- Increased ability to fall asleep (this could be due to a rise in Adenosine, which builds "Sleep Pressure")
\- Massively increased tolerance to Cold (collateral effect of the increase in Body Heat perhaps?)
\- Mental/Cognitive improvements
\- Increase in Dopamine, I always feel satisfied and rewarded afterwards, probably increased further by the Cold shower after too

**I'm keeping these up for the next year or so and seeing what happens long term. I have left some Extracts of 2 recent Studies of Finnish Men using Saunas which showed some HUGE statistical improvements for those using Saunas 2-3 times per week and 4-7 times per week in Cardiovascular Diseases, Dementia and All-Cause Mortality.**

[https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamainternalmedicine/fullarticle/2130724](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamainternalmedicine/fullarticle/2130724)

_"During a median follow-up of 20.7 years (interquartile range, 18.1-22.6 years), 190 SCDs, 281 fatal CHDs, 407 fatal CVDs, and 929 all-cause mortality events occurred. A total of 601, 1513, and 201 participants reported having a sauna bathing session 1 time per week, 2 to 3 times per week, and 4 to 7 times per week, respectively. The numbers (percentages) of SCDs were 61 (10.1%), 119 (7.8%), and 10 (5.0%) in the 3 groups of the frequency of sauna bathing. The respective numbers were 89 (14.9%), 175 (11.5%), and 17 (8.5%) for fatal CHDs; 134 (22.3%), 249 (16.4%), and 24 (12.0%) for fatal CVDs; and 295 (49.1%), 572 (37.8%), and 62 (30.8%) for all-cause mortality events. After adjustment for CVD risk factors, compared with men with 1 sauna bathing session per week, the hazard ratio of SCD was **0.78 (95% CI, 0.57-1.07) for 2 to 3 sauna bathing sessions per week** and **0.37 (95% CI, 0.18-0.75) for 4 to 7 sauna bathing sessions per week** (P for trend = .005). Similar associations were found with CHD, CVD, and all-cause mortality (P for trend ≤.005). Compared with men having a sauna bathing session of less than 11 minutes, **the adjusted hazard ratio for SCD was 0.93 (95% CI, 0.67-1.28) for sauna bathing sessions of 11 to 19 minutes** and **0.48 (95% CI, 0.31-0.75) for sessions lasting more than 19 minutes** (P for trend = .002); significant inverse associations were also observed for fatal CHDs and fatal CVDs (P for trend ≤.03) but not for all-cause mortality events."_

and the same group did another study but looking at Saunas and Dementia......

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27932366](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27932366)

_"during a median follow-up of 20.7 (interquartile range 18.1-22.6) years, a total of 204 and 123 diagnosed cases of dementia and Alzheimer's disease were respectively recorded. In analysis adjusted for age, alcohol consumption, body mass index, systolic blood pressure, smoking status, Type 2 diabetes, previous myocardial infarction, resting heart rate and serum low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, compared with men with only 1 sauna bathing session per week, **the HR for dementia was 0.78 (95% CI: 0.57-1.06) for 2-3 sauna bathing sessions per week** and **0.34 (95% CI: 0.16-0.71) for 4-7 sauna bathing** sessions per week. The corresponding HRs for Alzheimer's disease were 0.80 (95% CI: 0.53-1.20) and 0.35 (95% CI: 0.14-0.90)."_

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**Fasting (and diet)
**
Something else I've done in parallel to the Saunas is Fasting on a 23/1 protocol. I'm eating my food @ 4pm, and it is about 400g of Salmon or Halibut plus 60g of Coconut Oil and about 60g of Duck Fat.

The Fasting hasn't made me crash, which means my Cortisol is fine. I do get hunger at certain parts of the day, but I guess this is to be expected. I feel good post 14 hours of the fast, and then really fucking good at around 20 to 23 hours... quite literally high.

I played Football (soccer) fasted at 23 hours on Friday, and I played really well. I was the calmest member of the team, and was pulling the strings in midfield.

I'm definitely going to continue the 23/1 until I go to India and then I will scoff myself with Curry all day!

Since finding out that my nightly reactions are not really associated with Food, but are dependent on these mites, it means I have re-looked at foods I thought were "triggers" to my "eczema"...

I have been eating nothing but Fish, Fat and Eggs for the last 5 months, and as good as I feel I'm keen to see whether I can eat more food. I love food and also can I do a bit more "shit on a shingle" then I originally thought? Maybe I'm not allergic to everything? also, I'd like to put some weight on, and I'm so skinny ATM that I really want to be able to eat more!

So I've brought back in some Nuts and Dairy. 2 foods high in protein and fat, delicious and also big triggers for most Autoimmune diseases.

Almonds/ almond butter and Raw Unpasteurised Cream.... currently, I've had no issues, and I actually feel pretty good from them... no mental or cognitive issues and no gut issues, and no sleep issues. I will keep monitoring...

**Cold Thermogenesis
**
I'm daily CT'ing in the cold shower, twice a day. But it really doesn't feel enough. I feel like the Saunas have augmented my cold adaption somehow. But I can't get the effect off my shower anymore.

I have done several Ice Baths, at about 10°C or 50°F, and I do them at around 6am on a Fasted belly... I feel pretty good from them, and they increase my dopamine in replacement of the lowering AM sun.

I am finding it hard to do the Ice Baths daily though, I have to admit. My house the Bath tap is rubbish, it takes 30 minutes plus to run the bath, and the water isn't cold enough so I have to prep Ice Blocks by freezing Water in big containers. And some days, I wake up a little too late to do it. I will admit though, sometimes I just put myself off...... Trying to work on that.

**Water
**
I'm drinking Icelandic Glacial or Evian at the moment. The Icelandic definitely "feels" quite Deuterium Depleted... and it works very well on cold days when I've done Ice Baths. But I feel it does not work well on Sunny/Hot days.... I think this may be due to "over-depleting", and we do need some Deuterium in our Blood to make the UV-C light (VT 2018).

I'm not sure how it all works... but simply put, I drink Evian on sunny days, San Pellegrino on very sunny days, and Icelandic on cold/dark days.

**Thoughts and Plans
**
I have a trip to India for 3-4 weeks planned over xmas and I cannot fucking wait.

For the first time in a long time I am really feeling the Dopamine drop as the Sun is dropping here. Unless I'm busy, I can feel myself sliding towards depression again... nothing major, but certain tendencies return, and bad mental patterns.

Long term, I'm getting the fuck out of the UK. I'm witnessing how CT isn't good enough to offset the loss of Sun, and I'm also too fucking good to settle for that deal. Why should I stay here in this fucking despair pit with all these other zombies?

NO thankyou!

I'm glad I have options though, and these are my current thoughts:

1\. Finish my year at college, and take off Travelling round Asia/ Indonesia for a few months in 2019.
2\. While I'm out there, search for jobs in Thailand, Malaysia/Singapore, Cambodia and New Zealand.
3\. I am constantly bombarded by New Zealand immigration to recruit me to work over there..... They pay above and beyond because there is a shortage in my role over their
4\. I can work in Christchurch and then spend my weekends out in the Mountains (as far as I know, NZ is quite an optimal place - better than London anyway)
5\. Once I've collected some cash for 6 months I can take off again and fund another 6 months travelling asia and relaxing on the beach
6\. Or I get work in Asia, which is less demanding but a lot of construction going on - Cambodia/ Thailand/ Singapore is 10° N so very optimal for UV.
7\. I could do this On/Off work schedule for quite some time. I am lucky to have a job in a booming industry such as construction, and all over the world we are needed. But 6 months in New Zealand and 6 months in India or Thailand chilling on the islands doesn't sound a bad deal
8\. One day, I know that fun might run out... so I will be saving all the while
9\. I'm gonna start learning Italian. For some reason, my heart tells me that Italy might be the place I end up... Long term, I'm gonna steal Jack's idea and buy a farm somewhere. Maybe in Italy... who knows I could be wrong.
10\. There is good Sunshine there, and also lots of Farmland, it is also somewhere I resonate with culturally... Football, Great Food, History.... and some of the best beaches in the world.
11\. I'm also aware the Italy idea could be some low dopamine bullshit and never happen. But, we will see

I really want to dive into Dopamine and figure out how it all works... Jack says "light builds dopamine", but I can't find any actual evidence of how that works... But then, I've read Dopamine is increased by Light (not sure if they controlled for UV, I think they meant Bright Light - so probably an LED). I've also seen a study that says "striatal dopamine increased with more exposure to Sunlight" in a controlled study on women to look at effect of seasons on mood. There are lots of studies showing more Sunlight = more Dopamine in the eye.

So, if any Light increases dopamine, how does Blue specifically destroy it? and how does UV build it?

Also, Jack talks about the Absorbance Spectra of certain amino acids, one's like tyrosine (which makes dopamine) and tryptophan, phenylalanine and histidine all absorbing UV-C light..... but what is the relevance of that Biochemically?.... what does that translate to on the top level, more reactivity?

They have a Benzene ring, these Aminos. I asked my lecturer on Saturday how they work. So it is where in the structure of the molecule there is a ring of atoms all bonded, and light hits the electrons of these and can run in a ring, round and round this ring, jumping from electron to electron, effectively trapping the light.

So what though?!

Does that Light make those electrons more reactive or something? More excited? Then what effect does that have on the molecule's behaviour.... **does it mean better binding with other molecules?

I'm really keen to learn this detail so that I can speak to my fellow Biochem students and explain how Light hits these Aminos and makes more dopamine, serotonin, melatonin etc.

Because we spoke at length yesterday about how MTHFR cycle effects Dopamine and Serotonin production. And there was a line on the board going from Tyrosine to Dopamine and Tryptophan to Serotonin.

I really wanted to just scream out: "UV FUCKING LIGHTTTTT!".... but I didn't know the answers to potential questions... just a simple one like "so what they absorb light?".
