### Sean Waters — 2019-09-19T14:26:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278244>

I appreciate it J. I'm getting close to understanding it. I really am.

**I fully had it out, on the phone, with my friend who has just got back from the Canaries.
**
I spent time thinking about where my rationale was coming from, was it **_bad advice due to my own insecurities?
_**
I checked it, and checked it, and dug deeper into the reasons why I felt this way.

It all came back to feelings I felt after I'd have a conversation with him, during the depressing period of unemployment and low redox from February 2019 to May 2019. He would leave me drained. Why?

We would constantly debate, whether "following your passion" was something that **overcomes poor Redox**. So, if you did something you LOVED everyday, would that override any poor redox.

I started to realise, as my finances dwindled to lower and my debt increased, and I continued to try and become an "Online Health Coach", that following his rationale on "passion" was leading me into a hole.

I started to build Redox myself, more commitment on Sunrise and CT etc., and I realised that my "passion of helping people" did not DO ANYTHING for my own wellbeing or for my lust for life.

Sat in my home, on my laptop, making videos to people who were at work and too busy to give a fuck, while my own Redox dissipated under the WiFi and my lust for life squandered to fuck all as I lived out my 4 year "passion".

We had kinda stopped talking, because I was becoming more and more serious about NOT Following a passion and instead Embracing Discomfort...... for Redox, for Relationships, for Life.....

By Mid-July, I'd been to Mallorca and met the girl and had a magical experience with the One Zeros and then I'd been to Poland and been dressed down by Jack and felt in complete awe as he said "YOU ARE IN 2ND GEAR, YOU NEED TO STEP UP INTO A HIGHER GEAR THAT YOU DONT KNOW YOU HAVE"...........

Over the next month to Mid-August I'd found that Gear, week by week. I've delved deeper into myself as contact with him and my cocaine friend was severed to nothing........ my Redox has climbed to new heights, following my intuition, and entering into a tub of freezing water and being extremely committed to my goals.......

Through that, and my 9-5 office job...... I've found EXTREME PASSION AND LUST FOR LIFE........ Something that NEVER came from "doing my passion"........ I've actually FOUND THAT I HAVE MANY PASSIONS IF I WANT THEM AND I CAN CHOOSE WHAT I WANT TO ACT ON.... I FOUND ALL THIS FROM FINDING MYSELF, BURIED IN THE COMFORTABLE DARKNESS, AND PULLED HIM OUT AND THREW HIM INTO THE ICE AND INTO DISCOMFORT.....

Mid-September and as you all know from the forum I've been like a Fireball..... lighting up everyone around me and burning those who can't take my heat.

In the meantime, he has maxed out his final credit card and been declined any more loans. We get back in touch the last few days and he has called me out for having a "superiority complex", "not being able to take criticism", and "not being an entrepreneurial risk-taker, but an academic who needs structure like a 9-5 job", and "not understand he embraces the chaos of uncertainty by taking risks without having stable income".

I thought about all of those.......... and wondered if they had truth, or whether that was how we was framing it for his own peace of mind.

I've pretty much "Experienced" every single one of my health gains. I even leapt out of a job, girlfriend, house in london, and into a totally wild and uncertain reality in Mexico in 2018... I had no idea if i'd come back or what would happen.

What about his embracing of uncertainty? Is it accurate?

I think, maxing out credit cards, with the knowledge of bankruptcy saving you, is not embracing discomfort. I think, it is running away from the real chaos........ the Fear of a regular job, the fear of monotony, hard work...

When I asked him what he would do if Redox ever got so bad in UK winter what would he do?

"that is easy, I'll just top myself".

Hang on. So, you're ok with just passing off all your burden onto your family and friends by suicide.

Hang on. So, you're ok with just passing off all your needs to WORK and EARN money to feed yourself by taking out credit, and letting the tax-payer pay for it, instead of dealing with it yourself?

Hang on. So you're ok with not learning any of Jack's work yourself, but getting the information through me via questions on the phone each day?

Hang on. So you're not even willing to read books on Business/ Marketing or whatever you want to do for your passion, you just think "it's meant to be"?

Hang on. So, when you call me up saying you feel "unfulfilled" and need answers on why you feel low motivated but also unsure on your passion, but then refuse to accept it may be that you can't rely on passions for your fulfilment, it is because don't **embrace the suck** enough, you can't handle it and I'm an anti-entrepreneurial academic?

Hang on. So, when shit gets hard... you just bail and let other people deal with shit?

The picture is forming................. I've got someone, in my Circle, who IS NOT GOING ALL IN.

I've been vampired. Still, being vampired. Blood-sucked....... no wonder, my Redox has been amazing since we cut off, no wonder it was soooo bad when we were talking everyday..........

I got someone, in my circle, who is absolutely averse to everything I stand for.

He frames it as entrepreneurial....... but, whatever happened to doing your passion on the side, for 3 years, investing every spare penny of your salary, the hard-way, and making it fucking happen around a 12 hour shift and looking after 3 kids like [@Jenelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18778/) does..........

Whatever happened to 30 years of Neurosurgery, call @ night, Wife and kids, CT for 18 months straight to lose 147 pounds and teaching yourself Quantum Biology like [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) did???

Whatever happened to spending 5 years engineering a disease reversal, teaching yourself QB, working 8 til 5, doing a degree in your trade, doing a 2nd degree in nutrition to only cancel that cause it's fucking bullshit and then pay off the "pointless" debt by working in an office redox stealing job, and hacking it by CTing like a maniac, embracing the suck and teaching yourself Spanish, a Teach English course, AND setting up a Business for when you move to Central America LIKE I FUCKING DO...........

Man, this shit seriously, seriously, pissed me off when I started thinking about it.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-19T14:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278245>

On fire is an understatement.

Volcanic!

### 5G Canary — 2019-09-19T15:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278250>

Very inspiring Sean... I feel your heat! “Set your life on fire...seek those who fan your flames.”

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-19T15:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278251>

And after the phone call I've just had................... It is complete. It is fucking over.

He had a breakdown this morning, cried his eyes out at the sunrise, and rang me at lunch and crying his eyes out he said "I've avoided it, my whole life I've been running scared.... I've envied all the Boxers and Fighters my whole life because never been able to embrace the pain like they can..... I've ran from debt, ran from my problems.... I can't believe I was okay with killing myself, like what a fucking weak move.. such a weak fucking move, all the shit my family would have to go through...."

It was absolutely. Fucking. incredible.

He said....... "you went all-in for me. What would I have done, without you?... I'd be so fucked".

He couldn't understand why we had met, so randomly, in that Bali hostel 3 years ago... and then he corrected himself, "it is irrelevant isn't it?.... what does it matter how we met and the circumstances, if I don't act on it... all this universe stuff, the secret, the magic... it is all irrelevant if we don't act"

I told him that I believe Life doesn't take the path of least resistance. The Water carves its way through the Rocks and forms the paths it wants. We have the same power in our lives.

He basically, completely broke down, and admitted, he's been running away from pain his whole life. **I actually think he is fucking super-brave to do that. I told him, noone else I know has been able to have the bottle to admit this and do this. I told him, I had to push you extremely hard because otherwise I can't walk alongside you any further........ If you can do this, if you can overcome your fears, the World is yours and everything is in it..............

He self-destructed the "severed from the circle" button.... he said "I've been a vampire. I'll do it myself.... but I'll be back".

INCREDIBLE. I FUCKING LOVE YOU BRO.**

Sean,
I think everyone should learn to do just that, to be with one self, and just

STOP

for a while... before starting anything important, business, partnership etc.

We need to go inwards before we go outwards. Like a plant/tree. It has to grow roots before it grows on the surface. The most important stuff happens in darkness! Wow  just think about it

It is going to be a complete different thing when we do it this way.

Maybe we see there is no need to "go" anywhere! Who knows! But whatever happens, all moves we do will come from truth. It will be authentic. The opposite is what you see in your "friend" - how he acts and what he does.
It can be very eye opening to observe, and a great lesson.

I used to think I needed to "reach" somewhere too...lol
and then I realized, it was not about that at all   (and I am not saying we cant have goals and plans to do stuff. I am sure you know what I mean)

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-19T15:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278258>

It is actually, fucking, unbelievable............ and the relief I feel, man it is insane.......

And Inger - I know exactly what you mean. I've only learnt this recently......... we have to be warriors. We have to be able to take on any battle and any challenge and withstand the chaos. That includes being absolutely comfortable with yourself in any environment or any situation. That is the goal.

### Inger — 2019-09-19T15:46:12+0300

yep, Sean. Warriors.
Naked, burned black from the sun, wild and free.

wow  I love it.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-09-19T15:53:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278264>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=278258)
>
> It is actually, fucking, unbelievable............ and the relief I feel, man it is insane.......
>
> And Inger - I know exactly what you mean. I've only learnt this recently......... we have to be warriors. We have to be able to take on any battle and any challenge and withstand the chaos. That includes being absolutely comfortable with yourself in any environment or any situation. That is the goal.

**What is a Black Swan?**

You must leverage your strengths, and not just focus on repairing/fixing your weaknesses.
If you obsess too much on working on your opportunity areas it can grow tiresome--because it's tiring work. Doing the easy work matters too; that is, leveraging your natural strengths to make progress on things that matter. MOST. This includes being self-aware of both your strengths and your weaknesses and having self-compassion to be patient with the latter.

We can all too easily fall into the trap of lasering in on our weaknesses, ones exacerbated in our mind when we compare ourself to others. We’ll also find when we switch gears to leveraging our strengths, it's an effortless task because it renews the energy depleted in striving to get better at something we’re not so good at.

### caroline — 2019-09-19T21:54:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278294>

Do each of us know our own strengths? Our own weaknesses?

Years and years ago we were at a spa in Florida. One nite we went to a lecture by a very well know relationship therapist - to the stars apparently.

She asked each of us to tell our partner what we are best at. It was very, very surprising and revealing. I could not believe what my DH said he was best at. I was totally shocked ....not at all what I would have said.

I have thought about that evening many times.

The one huge take home for me ....she said “ everyone sees the world thru their own set of lenses - no one sees the world exactly like we do”

I have never forgotten that. I always try to remember it in difficult situations with my partner and others....

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-20T11:30:10+0300

The Lion doesn't try to run fast like the gazelle....... it just gets even better at hunting at night with its night vision.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T15:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278740>

**THE WISDOM OF REJECTION

So, the Carribean Opportunity #3 falls away with a firm "We don't want to interview him, thanks" from the Company........

This one was the job that had no "requirements" that may have pre-supposed a "No", and I was quietly confident this would be it.

Turns out... I was fucking wrong  The answer was a re-sounding plunge of rejection, deep into my gut. One that I couldn't make sense of...... until now.

**

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T15:43:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278741>

Today - we had the biggest Thunder and Lightning we've seen since last year... it was terrential fucking rain.

Last night, my Stepdads car nearly got robbed in the night.

My sleep was fucked - and my little bio-hacked Mattress idea using Futons has give me INSANE back ache.

Then, I get the call @ 11am which I was confident about.... "No thanks".

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T15:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278746>

I felt the recruiter's Pity, as another one slipped away, and my throat got tight, I felt saddened... and like when someone rejects you at school and you feel ugly and confused and want to know WHY... WHY? What is wrong with me, or my CV, or what?

And then I realised..............

I don't fully believe in myself. Someone, or anyone, has the power over me to make me feel this way. **And this... is my Chaos of Rejection... the Chaos of Self Belief.

I mentioned already, I have my Teach English certificate nearly there. I also have an Entrepreneurial idea that I was so excited for and ALL IN for until last week when this "Financially Stable, Same Skill, Ideal world job in the tropics appeared......."
**
I should have known from [@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) and [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) that this wasn't a good idea to follow... you guys predicted it. But, it slowly increased in its power over me, as I imagined how Ideal it would be if I got it.
**
I was drawn to it.... drawn to how "perfect" it would be to get it...... I'd have all this fucking cash, tax-free, I can do whatever, I'll be at the 12th latitude for Sunrise, this will be insane, I can build the business on the side.....

...... and quietly I said and ignored my own alarm bells on the idea: "I'll just have to be alone for like 6-12 months in my apartment, yeah I won't be hanging with the Mito crew but it's worth it...... and I can't really go and spend next Summer in Canada with my girl, but I can do regular 4-5 day trips, and I'll still be able to go Mexico to the event... yeah it is way better".
**
**IS IT THOUGH SEAN?........... Or are you just choosing Comfort, over embracing your Fears.........**

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T16:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278748>

I spent my whole lunch hour thinking about it, under the cloudy rainy sky.... I started to understand I actually don't have Self-Belief 100% there.

And, considering what I have done... what I am able to do... It is complete unjustified.

I keep delaying Self-Belief. Like it will come from doing a "thing" or an event in the future.

**I am done with delaying it. It is time to breath fire into my own Cooking Pot......

So FUCK THE COMPANY. They made a huge error not employing me. But I'm grateful for their mistake. Because it has threw me into some Chaos that I needed to go through...

If I get take the risk, and make my own company, and make my own reality happen - without assistance from a nice comfortable full time job - I can have the whole world and more - I am fully free....... I just have to back myself, One Hundred Fucking Percent, to make it happen.**

### Inger — 2019-09-24T16:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278752>

Sean... what if the meaning of all this is...you just might want to stop for a bit
Maybe that is what you need? Sometimes it is....
Just stop, and feel and taste whatever is around, fully

I think in our "doing" oriented culture it is not appreciated to stop. That is a mistake. Because that can be of immense value - to do that for a while-, even if it seems like nothing! The clue is, being aware and present doing it, taste every little ting, every flavor.. every feeling....
Wintertime is great for that

Maybe I am all wrong and you have another mission!

But for me this stopping feels better and better! I just want to check out and meet this little girl Inger, who is she?
Just dating myself a little while... is a very cool thing
there is a different kind of life, of joy, that is emerging inside
And I have to stop and wonder, wow.. how do I feel so safe?! And nothing is happening, no plans, no one around, I am 100% alone and responsible for everything in my life. Bills, job, car, food, shelter.... but that does not disturb me. I used to feel fear of not making it alone. But that fear, where did it go?  So weird...lol. It was not pleasant at first, it was like stopping a drug abuse. Yeah!

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T16:46:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278753>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=278752)
>
> Sean... what if the meaning of all this is...you just might want to stop for a bit
> Maybe that is what you need? Sometimes it is....
> Just stop, and feel and taste whatever is around, fully
>
> I think in our "doing" oriented culture it is not appreciated to stop. That is a mistake.

I agree. Deep down, I know what you are saying is truth.

But... I don't think _**I want that**_... despite the fact, it would "release me" from desires and jobs and plans.....

I think I want those things. I think I want a challenge...... perhaps, a challenge is just another addiction, like the smartphone, like food, like sex, like drugs......

But then again, do Animals out there **enjoy the hunt? enjoy the kill?
**
Or do they just merely do it to eat............

I often think, how long would it take for me to adapt to being truly Wild and Alone, like yourself. And if, ultimately, I would just be completely at peace, and all of this erroneous bullshit I've invented to chase after is just completely blocking me from true peace within.. and whatever that could bring.

**The answer... Right now... I don't know.**

**But mainly... Right now, I don't want to embrace that, either way. And that, most importantly, is my choice, and a choice I'm happy with...**

### Inger — 2019-09-24T16:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278756>

if you do not feel like doing it, you should not.
But this was my biggest challenge..... how paradox that sounds
and it stopped that inner suffering. This anxious looking for fulfillment outside myself, huh!
And now, I have tons of fun
I now think life is meant to be enjoyed!
at work they often ask, Hey what drugs are you on?
I love my work BTW. I am now 5 years at the same place, and really love it

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T17:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278757>

I agree. Although, I can completely see your point. It isn't quite resonating right now... I think that is important too, Timing... and timing of your Chaos...

I do believe we need to grow. Even if, ultimately, I grow into what you describe, perhaps I need to take the long hard road round for all the lessons buried in it. I don't think I can switch on the "just be" button.

So you do find enjoyment in your work........... I think that is important. I think Labour, and working, is really important. I'm not sure why, but again, Fulfilment... Reward... the Hunt.. the Kill. It makes you Value life when you must work for it.

Spending 2 months, on the beach, in Isla Mujeres last year did nothing but drive me insane with boredom.

Why did I feel like I needed to work or do something?

Is it low dopamine... or is it because we are supposed to work?

### Inger — 2019-09-24T17:32:37+0300

well Sean, what I mean is not doing nothing, it is an inner thing.

It is not so much about what you do or dont do.

date
yourself

### MITpowered26 — 2019-09-24T17:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278761>

This is a key learning I’m going through right now as well. Stoicism. To still have the emotions and thoughts you are having, Sean, but add a layer of stillness to them.. a layer of observation... a layer of mindfulness. Only then, I feel, I personally will be able to listen deeply within to make “better” decisions that help me reach where my soul needs to ultimately reach - more efficiently?

### MITpowered26 — 2019-09-24T17:50:45+0300

### Inger — 2019-09-24T18:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278765>

I love how Osho says it, we should make as many mistakes as possible, but never repeat the same twice! Because that makes one stupid.

On another note... unresolved trauma, or grief or any other emotional wounds that are not healed, if we do not heal them (and as adults, we can!), they will just stay under the surface and act through our unconscious as a ghost.. creating the same struggle and pain again and again.
This is what happened to me.
So I just realized I had to do something different!
It worked!

### drezy — 2019-09-24T18:45:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278769>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=278757)
>
> Why did I feel like I needed to work or do something?

Have you consider that maybe you've just got ants in your pants?

I do and I'm not even wearing pants right now, thought you should know...

I've always been active and I don't think it will change. I think I'd have classified it as restless in my 20s. The 20s can be a restless time. I did sit with a group in my mid 20s for almost 2 years. I think it has value and it did get integrated into me somehow.

Take stock of what you have gained in order to slow your despair and panic. So far in your 20s you've learned:

-   How you break
-   How you mend
-   That the "I need to GTFO" you emerges under conditions (and may be insightful)
-   In other conditions you want to entertain self-doubt
-   a shmillion other minor details

I honestly still wouldn't bet against you with what you've learned and your greatest asset of time/youth.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T19:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278773>

I think much of what you guys observe in my writing is because of the environment and redox I'm in:

_**100+ Wifi Office Block 9-5 and an Electrified House full of Tech @ the 52nd Latitude with fuck all Sunshine, H1 middle eastern mitochondria and some Dopamine SNPs.**_

The urge to Get Out and to push forward is because I know I'm running low on time, subconsciously and consciously.......

There are often times I feel still and calm. Sunrise and Sunset, everytime, outside of the power grid.

But even then, sometimes I think of the future, and I plan and plan and plan.

The last 2 years have been traumatic. Honestly, I've had so much emotional and physical fucking trauma with friends, family, girlfriends etc. that I'm fucking done wasting time on anything but getting out of this hell-hole.

26 years I've spent, being sick. It is time to get out. It is time enough.

Perhaps once I'm in Mexico for a while, I will naturally feel the stillness you speak of in my thoughts...... As I say, I get it here in states of high redox, i.e. Sun and CT times.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-24T19:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-278774>

I don't feel such Panic though. Honestly... I'm just someone who thinks and acts at high intensity.

I don't think focusing on that intensity being bad, is a good idea (lasering on weaknesses), but instead focus on how good it is for me (focus on strengths).

I've achieved what I've achieved on this journal, due to my erratic, impulsive and high intensity behaviour..... I've smashed into walls of chaos and times where it really sucked, due to not having such stillness.... but through it, I'm gaining more control and understanding than ever.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-27T17:51:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279054>

Lost my head this AM with my Exec manager.

This happened before at my last project last year.

I don't feel embarrassed this time (Win). But, I know I've only got 7 weeks to keep my head down and get out here with plenty of cash to take the leap to Mex.

I'm just practicing being aware of my emotions right now. I can't quite decide if I made a good decision today to do that.

I think, I'm learning the consequences of being "Firey". There are consequences. You can hurt people. Upset your own position.

But then, what do you tolerate and what do you not?

I didn't insult him. But my shortness, directness, in my emails were enough to show my frustration to everyone.

As of today I hit my target for Debt paid off and I'm in the Plus on my finances.

But if I make the 7 weeks I got a stack to relax on.

Why am I risking that?... these problems aren't even mine. I'm a contractor. Why am I getting involved?...

I think I'm rattled biologically... upset Gut... my environment... the Sun has dropped significantly........

The Cold Tub seems to emphasise my Boldness and my rage.

Onions too - they really get me firing mentally.

Pondering..... **not self-doubting..** pondering.

### Jenelle — 2019-09-27T20:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279072>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279054)
>
> Onions too - they really get me firing mentally.

I had a sinus infection that got so bad recently ... I finally gave in and took 5 days of a hefty antibiotic. It was the first antibiotic I'd had in 2.5 years ~ and I don't know when was the last time I had one before that. (Long).

Anyway -- I don't know what the deal is with **onions**! I decided quite awhile ago that they upset my digestion too much and are NOT worth it ... but ever since I had that sinus infection and started/finished the antibiotic, I have _very noticeably_ gravitated toward onions -- CRAVED onions (as part of a meal, not by themselves) -- raw, cooked, both, doesn't matter -- and I've been going ahead and eating them.

...I just decided not to worry about my digestion ~ and started wearing some adult absorbent briefs I got from work.

^ kidding. It hasn't been that bad.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-28T19:01:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279147>

Alright so update on that ^^

My Candles burned down to the brim and I had to use my Red Incandescent Bulbs that i'd not used in probably 3 or 4 months after I'd cleared my room out of all electrics/ metal objects **to lower the E-Fields
**
I'd kinda said "fuck it" to Artificial Light, including Red - even from little incandescents.

To be honest, I hadn't noticed any difference over the summer.

But obviously we hit Autumn (official) last week and the Red/ IR light has dropped and I've lost a good 90 minutes in total off Sunrise and Sunset before/ after work.... **so** **on a relative basis, my Blue Light to Red Light has increased dramatically.

How is Light sculpting my life these days?........ Blue = Pro-Inflammatory.

I had quite a lot of what Jenelle is describing from the Onions, but also a lot of "hot" thinking too.

I literally had 2 hours from 7-9pm (after sunset) of Red Light Incandescent Bulbs instead of Candles last night and I noticed a major difference in my thinking and my gut.

This morning, while making breakfast around all the E-Fields and Mag Fields of the Kitchen, which usually leads to _more inflammatory thinking_ about my family/ parents... I had my Red Lights on.... and lo and behold... I felt very relaxed and I didn't get that issue.

However - one thing I must add. I did test the E-Fields around the bulbs themselves... and about 1 metre radius = some high readings for an EHS primate... so I'm keeping my distance.
**
Heading towards the Winter Solstice.... I think some Added Red Light is a good idea.

It is interesting how when the Wi-Fi was still on at the house back before Summer, I was using these Lights ALOT but never noticed their effect either.

It is only until the RadioFrequency is turned OFF... Sun has really fell off... AND my Redox (CT etc.) has picked up... that I'm able to notice some improvements with the Red Light.

Light is such a touchy, sensitive, specific arena when it comes to Health. Making quick presumptions regarding Light is a BIG risk for Humans.... it is STILL in my Dunning Kruger, but a little less after this revelation.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-28T19:04:47+0300

I've only got 7 weeks left til I get some Mexican Red Light for the rest of my life...

the thought of that is actually starting to become REAL in my mind.

I'm actually feeling the separation from Parents, Family, Friends in my stomach and mind as a real feeling now... I can remember it from last time, but this time is more pronounced because it really is for good this time.

Coming back is NOT. AN. OPTION.

All my Bio-hacks, Diet, Light, CT, no WiFi (thanks to Mom), no phone, no socialising, no bad food, not a single drop of alcohol, no being out after sunset (7pm now) etc. are literally holding my health up - mainly Mentally - on the tip of a fucking knife edge.

It is all working though. I'm balancing well on that edge. In fact, i feel pretty fucking good.... but one small error and I'm annihilated and in panic mode once again because I can't get it back... I can't think... I can't feel good.

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-28T19:15:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279154>

For some reason, I've stopped thinking of Mexico as just "Health, Crater, Sun"....

I've had images come to mind lately, popping in at Sunset and Sunrise normally (downloading those One Zeroes)....

The Talavera Pottery, the Day of the Dead, the food, the nature and the history... even the criminal aspects, the cartel, the crime and this rich history of the country....

Basically... it has massive culture... and it is like I'd forgot about all of this, and it has completely renewed my Excitement and sense of purpose for this trip (not so much for the Cartel).

**My canadian girl has insisted I learn Salsa... she can salsa, and she's a Latin American enthusiast, and can speak Spanish... so she's kicking me up the arse to get up to scratch on these things... that's what best friends are for right?

She's also coming to the Mexico Christmas event, and bringing her friend who has Chronic Fatigue/ EHS and M.E. symptoms.... she's going on the 3rd, but her friend is staying out.

We are going to Chiapas for a week alone, and then coming back to Cancun for Christmas.

I can't wait.... tick tock tick tock...**

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-30T16:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279238>

Wild weekend........

**Mother**
We went to Salsa but we got the wrong day. Dumb fucks.

So we ended up driving round for 1 hour talking 1 to 1, with noone else listening (Stepdad).

She is really turning it around. She's had honest talks with her own Mother (childhood trauma/ resentment) and my Sister, and said she's had the best days with those two in years because of the released tension.

She has recognised her impatience/ disrespect to others. She has recognised her ignorance. But above all, she's recognised her little she values herself.

We are starting to see the true Vampire/ Disease in our family. It is my Step-dad.

He came in at a time when my Mom was financially fucked, 3 kids all at school, working 4 jobs. He gave the security and she let him take control.

He's a grown child. He creates a massively toxic atmosphere, we have a web of lies around him because anything he finds out he uses against her. He wields money like a weapon against her.

She's actually running his business, walking his dogs, doing his dishes, dinners, going where he wants on weekends and she says she gets drunk because of how bored she gets.

She's SHIT SCARED of leaving him but she's a brave woman and I think the seed is planted..... when I leave, she will have no kids to "provide for" anymore.... to be honest, I never take a penny from her, but she will be alone with him then and I think that will be the catalyst for her.

I showed her that **_she isn't the Woman that married him...... you aren't a Bar-maid anymore Mom.... you're a Company Director, a HR director... you can get employed ANYWHERE for 70, 80, 90k a year....

She said "oh... fuck... I can can't I?"

It was like she'd never thought she was worth anything by herself. Not "like", she actually hadn't._**

She realised... when I said "Every Saturday you've wasted with this ignorant pig you've lost... never can get a refund... you've lost them all... and everyone in the future, will never be given back.... ARE YOU OK WITH THAT?"

"No.... no I'm not Sean".

**Round of applause for my Mother. She's embracing the fucking chaos.

We got home and hugged for the first time and I didn't feel that "pull" in my gut. I didn't feel like I didn't mean it. It felt genuine and true.

Having massive, hurtful, brutally honest, upsetting and explosive arguments is the new Bio-Hack in my life........ It is fucking unbelievably good for Relationship Redox.**

**The level of fucking Power I now Wield......... worth, every, fucking, second, of boredom.**

### Sean Waters — 2019-09-30T16:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279243>

**My Boss (as per argument on Friday)
**
We had the sit down today....... As I said on Friday, in the morning I'd gone off on one and lost my temper at his demands/ queries.

My thinking was: Why am i getting worked up at a Freelance/ Contractor Role when it ain't even my fucking issue?

My answer/ conclusion: Because I care about every part of my life, and I won't settle for less just because. If someone steps out of line, or is unfair, it doesn't matter the circumstances - I value myself too highly to tolerate it.

The Result:
\- He saw how much i "cared" and loved it... **therefore, not putting my job in jeopardy but ACTUALLY INCREASING my ties with the Head of my department and giving me a firmer hold on my position which is temporary**
\- He signed off some of the forms, because of the pressure I put on
\- He gives me more respect
\- He thanked me, for what I did and how I acted
Understanding
\- Better relationship than before

After the meeting today, I left with my head held high. After my colleagues commented beforehand: "time for you to get your arse kicked".

No actually guys... You got your arses kicked. I stood my ground. I still didn't back down. And I got the best result.

He said "I know how much you care."

What do they say?..... **it ain't about how much you know, but how much you care... that is all people want to know.**

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) No I don't think you get what I'm saying...... **_I think you are still running away from Life. I think your "Just Be" is how you escape from doing what really scares you._**

Your advice to me last week was in direct response **to me finding Self-Belief after Rejection**. Instead of supporting me to believe in myself, you chose to tell me that I needed to "Just Be" and slow down from all this thought of seeking out Riskier Business than a safe job in the caribbean. **My post said: my caribbean job = security and safety. My rejection from the job was a lesson for me to LEARN how to not feel rejected and BELIEVE IN MYSELF but also STOP being AFRAID of the risks of my business idea and stop seeking comfort.**

**_Why?... Why does me finding Self-Belief unsettle you?

Why does me seeking out a riskier, entrepreneurial position unsettle you vs comfort?

Why did you feel you needed to defend your position on Meditation yesterday... my post wasn't @ you, so why did you need to step in and justify yourself?

Is my pursuit of Risks, Passion and Adventurous Living, making you feel uncomfortable because it no longer gives you an excuse to ignore it in your own life?_**

I think the Fears and Demons that I'm embracing recently on this Journal (in my life) is unsettling you and your position. Your position of comfort. The island you have created for yourself.

You are the wildest woman on this forum Inger, but yet are you missing a life of passion?

Is that your chaos?

The naked sunbathing, being the weird one at work, the fermented foods... could that actually be your comfort... your escape?

**Where were you in Poland Inger?... or Flowfest, Munich, on your doorstep?... Still radio silence on you coming to Mexico?........**

You don't Fear being alone with your simple job and simple life where noone can disrupt you or test you. What you Fear is the life of Chaos. **Your Fear is what I am embracing myself.**

**Self-Belief**. Confidence amongst the masses. **Risks**. Walking into Chaos and not being afraid.

It isn't just you either. [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) is the same... he told me "don't you think you've got ants in your pants...." basically, I'm restless and that he got therapy for it in his 20s (insinuated that might be wise for me).

He also said; "give thanks for what you have learned... you have the map of your breakdown...", firstly I think that's a good comment. But, in the context of it... it is actually about telling me to be happy with what I have, to slow down.

**Why are you telling me to slow down? Is it for my benefit... or is it for yours?

Does it make you nervous that I'm willing to tackle my Fears and demons head on?... Does it remove the excuses you give yourself for doing the same?**

**You both discouraged me from Growth. We should never discourage our friends from Growth.
**
Dan's reference to Crabs in a Bucket recently towards my Cocaine friend was relevant, but did he realise that he is doing it himself to me and others on this very journal?

When was the last time you embraced your own Fears and took leaps Dan? How about you Inger?

When was the last time either of you stepped off your Islands and safe-havens and into uncharted territory?

It is for this reason I cannot take either of your advice. I don't want to slow down, just be, or stop being restless. I think all of those things make both of you, and many others on this forum, very uncomfortable.

The reason I can't take your advice, is because
a) I believe it may come from your own desires to slow me down to make you feel better.
b) Neither of you have Skin in The Game on this topic as neither of you are living a Passionate life where you truly embrace your fears and attack them. And if you do, which I'm unaware of as I only have your activity on here for reference... **_then you wouldn't discourage me from taking leaps forward in my Thinking or my Goals, no matter how fast I was moving in my 20s..._**

I appreciate both of you have great knowledge on self-healing, and I don't wish to disrespect you or insult you as I like you both very much. I'd consider Dan a close friend lately. But this whole scenario has made me re-think.

On this topic, I reject your advice. But I thank you both for the lesson you gave me.

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-02T13:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279372>

[https://medium.com/@aishabeau/the-mindset-shift-that-helps-me-deal-with-rejection-f4ca9c92ddee](https://medium.com/@aishabeau/the-mindset-shift-that-helps-me-deal-with-rejection-f4ca9c92ddee)

" “You are going to know failure if you’re brave with your life” is a quote from Michael Bernard Beckwith that stood out to me during an uncertain time in this journey.

When I heard him recite these words, something inside of me shifted.

It became clear that being rejected-which was happening _a lot_ on this new adventure-wasn’t the end of my story. It was actually proof that I was stepping outside of my comfort zone to reach the goals and opportunities I desired.

Yes, rejection wasn’t what I wanted, but it also showed that I was challenging myself.

Suddenly, I felt empowered by my rejections.

**It’s no secret that rejection hurts.**
When we’re rejected, it can quite literally cause us pain.

[Studies have found](https://www2.psych.ubc.ca/~schaller/308Readings/DeWall2011.pdf) that the brain regions that are activated as a result of being rejected are the same areas that light up on fMRI scans in response to physical pain" Letoya Gaines Psy.D., a clinical psychologist.

The sting of rejection is literally real. This is why, for many of us, it feels better to simply avoid situations that come with a chance of rejection-at all costs.

But what if you were to tweak your mindset, looking at not the loss but the _gain_ that comes from rejection? Rejection can actually be the catalyst for something greater in your life.

“Because we can’t predict the future, there is no guarantee that the job you got rejected from was going to be the perfect fit,” Gaines says. “Rejection could be an opportunity to go after something else that could be much better in the long run.”

**The greatest measure of rejection’s impact is in how we react to it or let it affect us.**
Rejection’s pain loses steam when we acknowledge it and use it as the spark to aim higher, dream bigger, and push ourselves further.

Realistically, this can be a little daunting. But if you practice stepping out of your comfort zone, a little bit at a time, and embracing what comes your way as a result-the fear of rejection becomes less discouraging."

Fucking love it.

Embrace the Chaos of Rejection.

........ _Rejection’s pain loses steam when we acknowledge it and use it as the spark to aim higher, dream bigger, and push ourselves further.........._

### Jack Kruse — 2019-10-02T14:20:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279373>

My vaccine for rejection is Nature.

What gives you solace? After years in utter darkness littered by artificial light, I've forced my eyes into the light of the sun. As long as there is a sunrise in the world, how bad could things be really? I believe Nature is the solace for my soul. This solace place isn't a hiding place. It is a finding place for me. It is a place where I can create who I want to be. In essence, with reflection, I now see Nature has become my anchor, my solace, my opiate of choice. She has become my port of call.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-02T14:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279374>

Sean, i’m not sure if you noticed nearly as much as I did ~ but as I was in nursing school, there was very little support here for that decision ...

There are many reasons for this. And I feel like I understood all of them ... yet I still had to move forward with my plan. I knew what I needed, and what my family needed, in a way that no one else here could possibly understand. I knew that I could type all day long every day, and it was not going to change the minds of those who were discouraging me.

As great as this forum is, AND IT IS — and I am thankful for it every day — It still has its own limitations. It is not the same as meeting someone in real life, or even over a FaceTime call. It is not the same as getting to know someone — REALLY getting to know someone.

Becoming a nurse did not lock me into a box away from the sun, being stuck at a desk under blue lights, stressed out, pulling my hair out all day long for the rest of my life.

Becoming a nurse FREED me from the box I had already been trapped in for many, many years. It awakened a part of me that I had almost forgotten was even there. It has made me — and continues to make me — overcome numerous fears and anxieties, to get out there in the real world and \*let my light shine\* every day I go to work.

I face rejection, and judgment ... and I deal with looking like a dumbass sometimes, when I know I’m really not.

I rise above it. I keep moving forward. I learn. I learn from everything. I take things away from these experiences that no one else can imagine or cares about.

So what I’m saying is, when you know something deep down, to the core of your very being ... when you know that you are making the right choice for YOU, and that it is something you MUST do ... you will do it. It’s that simple. And it does not matter if anyone else — anywhere — understands it or not.

I was still going to become a nurse, no matter what anyone here said. But it will never be forgotten that you are the person here who told me: “Can imagine you will be SUCH a fucking epic nurse”.

That gave me fuel for the fire that was already there. It helped me burn brighter. And that FIRE is now spreading, and touching the lives of others, and making a difference, no matter how small.

So I will encourage you to pursue your passions. Know that there are those who will discourage you, and I believe here on the forum they have the best of intentions. I really do. It is a great group of people, and I am thankful for all of you every day. But also, continue to have the confidence to charge forward with something you know in your heart. No one here can see your heart. That one is your very own.

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-02T15:42:54+0300

As long as there is a sunrise in the world, how bad could things be really? I believe Nature is the solace for my soul. This solace place isn't a hiding place. It is a finding place for me. It is a place where I can create who I want to be. In essence, with reflection, I now see Nature has become my anchor, my solace, my opiate of choice. She has become my port of call.

With her behind you, you can do anything...... Nature provides the Light we need to create Art in our lives. If we do not use that creativity to its full potential, if we never leave our comfort zone, then that Gift we have everyday is wasted...

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-02T15:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279385>

They do have the best intentions. And I've highlighted Dan and Inger but what they've given me in advice in the past has been absolutely spot on. But it isn't just them.... it is a mindset, an attitude, that is present among many in this community and seemingly everyone around me.

**I don't expect anyone to take up how I choose to go through life. I don't even expect them to agree on what I've said.

How they live, their choices, everything they do is up to them.**

But I had to step in and defend my position on this. Even if it is only right for myself. This is my journal... and the advice last week had really gotten to me and I've spent a long time thinking about it before I acted.

I hope they can see what I've said as a "Sting" of Rejection, and that it will make them re-evaluate and perhaps see things in a different light... I definitely do not intend to cause strife or harm... but sometimes it is inevitable, as I've learnt lately.

**Explosive, hurtful, brutally honest, raging arguments are my new Bio-Hack... I said that the other day.**

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-02T16:00:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279388>

[@Jenelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18778/) and you made a choice and acted on it for something you wanted in life, whether it was risky, full of artificial light, dangerous for your Redox, you did it anyway.... and that is what I think truly finding yourself after a Disease is all about...

I think life goes beyond Epi Paleo and CT and the protocols.

As we see in Jack's blogs.... Relationships and Growth and Passion are the next level to Optimal Health. But, they are actually the most **difficult and most scary tasks**, as they involve getting into the CT Tub internally... diving into your fears and attacking them.

Those Fears will be different for each person.

I'm glad I had that effect on you, I barely remember the comment, but I'm pretty sure I probably told you to be careful with your Redox as that was my frame of reference back then.

But I've grown since then, and I'll grow again, and my mind will change again.

So you may change your mind on what you want, and that is fine too, or you will finish up as head of your hospital or more!... but no matter what, you are Growing and breaking down your own barriers week in and week out.

That is what matters most to me. That is the life I want!

### drezy — 2019-10-02T17:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279403>

There are two old stories that better summarize what I am trying to convey:
"This too shall pass"
and this one read by none other than good old Watts
[**https://youtu.be/byQrdnq7\_H0**](https://youtu.be/byQrdnq7_H0) (The story of the Chinese farmer)

You and I both grew up with plenty of opportunity for and examples of "going nowhere'. Something clicks and you want out and you're young and you've never really felt this before and it's uncomfortable.

I’m not advising that you stop any of your actions. If taking stock of your gains seems defeatist to you there may be something worth thinking about there. I'm not suggesting stopping. Refocusing is worth it though. Sports teams huddle and military squads regroup or switch to plan b in order to consolidate gains and proceed.

From what I read it seemed like you were experiencing some level of despair. I may have misread that based on my experiences at the time. That was what I was worried about.

All advice is what people are sharing from their experiences. You need to balance it in your own head and decide if it applies now, later, or never.

My experience is that waves of chaos come and go. Restlessness, discomfort, and the big green rage monster don’t go away. They can become useful tools though. If it was despair, pushing through it and having a plan is very useful.

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279370)
>
> When was the last time you embraced your own Fears and took leaps Dan?

One month ago.

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279370)
>
> When was the last time either of you stepped off your Islands and safe-havens and into uncharted territory?

ditto

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279370)
>
> . [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) is the same... he told me "don't you think you've got ants in your pants...." basically, I'm restless and that he got therapy for it in his 20s (insinuated that might be wise for me).

The next sentence I claimed that I also have ants in my pants. It's a badge not meant in a derogatory sense. It's a built in "not settling" that when focused is useful and not to be discarded.

It wasn't therapy. A hot girl I worked with went to a Buddhist group. So I figured I'd have a look see. I "got the girl" in pretty short order and stuck around a while because the older nun who ran the group regularly requested being driven in my sports car. She told me stories of her hellion past and how she always like speeding in sports cars. She'd would turn my radar detector on, look out for cops and "woo hoo" at high speeds. It was an odd and unique enough reality with such and interesting character I hung around for a while.

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-02T19:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279421>

Your Post Screamed that I need to stop worrying about this pursuit of my soul. Whatever/ how you justify it, it was not there to encourage me on that train of thought.

I thought I was confused, but I actually don't think either of you really understand WHY you wrote those things...

**I'm giving you a mirror, in these posts, to look into WHY you felt that way.**

**My path on this journal was to optimal health, but now it is that I need to stop being scared, and take on the unknown - that will truly give me the life of adventure I desire, and settling for the same will not.**

You both have a huge amount figured out in controlling your Life and your Environment and your Redox,
**
But is it too controlled?**

I don't think you really get what I'm feeling right now. It is an intensity and lust for life I've not had ever before. Until you've felt it, I don't think you can understand. It is embracing the pain of any situation. It is actually turning every Fear around in your mind and making it into something you seek out.

I'm pushing you right now to seek that out with this Mirror. If you don't want to look, that is fine for you....... But I want to look at my own, and I want to pursue my fears...... it ain't restless, it ain't despair, it is full throttle attack on myself and those around me.

Maybe I should leave everyone alone?........ or maybe those who don't like it should stay clear of me?

And you say....... This too, shall pass...... **Or maybe it doesn't.** Because maybe I'm making the Choice to be on Fire my whole life.
So don't ever discourage me from that. My mantra on life right now is this:

**We should encourage everyone to take risks and strides forward, which means pushing them to confrontation of Fears. We should encourage intensity and fire my friend, and actually it is even better when you add your own Fire to the mix.**

I think this is what the Circle of Six is all about, to me anyway.

As your Friend, I've been as Honest as I can with how I feel about this. I've held nothing back. I don't know how accurate it is, but you will know if it strikes a chord within you.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-03T20:07:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279498>

Perhaps a tangent, but I’m curious if any of this anger and rage comes from generally being British, stuck on those little islands with fuck all vitamin D, watching so many colonies drift away over the years and (for better or worse) rampant immigration from all angles?

My British friend never ceased to amaze me at how he could alternate between uber-polite and insanely crass, often within the same sentence. He was dealing with loss of control of his body so I usually gave him a pass, but then I encountered a few more Brits who did the same thing. It was explained to me that that’s just how British friends treated each other and it was understood that most of the nastiness was in jest, and it was all a game of one-upmanship in witty wretchedness.

It seemed to exacerbate after the brexit vote, and the utter chaos that consumed those beautiful and mighty lands.

Just an observation.

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-04T11:22:52+0300

But his Mom agreed to turn the WiFi off a few weeks back... **the environment significantly improved** for both. With the improved environment their relationship improved along with their redox.

**But it was still bad for 7 weeks after that was turned off........** It wasn't until I used my Rage and my Anger on her in the house that day that she actually got the message. She fully, felt the heat of my rage and it made her wonder.... "shit... why is he so fucking angry at me?".

That moment provides clarity.

The one thing she has said lately, is how much clarity this whole process has gave her.

Unfortunately, for people who can't handle the heat - it upsets them.

But having Crohn's disease upset me. EHS upset me. Chronic Fatigue upset me. Depression upset me.

But where would I be without all of that pain, that made me find my own clarity and my own mission?

Pain and suffering are commonplace in nature.

It is about time people on here, and out there, started embracing it.

I don't see what I've said here the last few weeks as nastiness. I see it as being absolutely clear to people what I truly think and what they are missing.

If yo don't like it, you don't have to interact with me. But if you want some truth in your life, and you want to grow... step right in.

Page 0-44 of this journal were about finding my fire.

Page 45- onward is about using that fire.

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-07T12:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279726>

**So how did me and my Mom break down another Wall of Fear within us yesterday?

SALSA.
**
After literally being told "I had to" learn Salsa before I hope to spend Xmas with her in Mexico, I felt pretty motivated and challenged by my girl in Canada.

I have to say, she's like the perfect match for me right now... She loves anything Latin American - Salsa, Food, the People, the Art, the Culture. Plus, she speaks spanish and dances well.

She even had 3 girls fly in from Costa Rica she met travelling to Montreal and stayed with her.

She is pushing me. Harder and harder. But not aggressively, but leading me with her own capability. Inspiring me. Making me want to catch up, to be able to walk with her with my head held high.

Something I've never, ever, ever, been comfortable with - Dancing.

I'm the guy who leans against the wall and says "Salsa is for fags" while the hot dude is killing it and every woman is watching him move.

It is so obviously insecurity and lack of self-confidence that it isn't even worth analysing what that is.

Once again......... it is Fear.

But would you expect, my own Mom to have the same Fear?

I took her up on Salsa, and neither of us wanted to go last night, we knew we should but we both talked about bailing pretty much the whole way there.

At the Car Park, I managed to get us past the urge to drive back and into the building.

We walk in, and I see about 15 women, doing the Mambo up and down, Salsa Styling... my god, my entire mammalian reproductive male survival mode mechanism of "Abort. Abort" kicked in to avoid potential embarrassment in front of all these females.

At that point, my Mom decided to take the lead. She said "come on then", even though she was dead nervous.

She's very glamorous, very attractive woman. She works so hard on her appearance, yet still feels inadequate and shy. She really doesn't know her own worth. Do I? (Mirror).

So we stood in the corner watching until the Beginner's class. I couldn't believe how good they looked. It was incredible.

Then, we all got in and did a big dance to warm up. The music come on and I couldn't help but smile, I felt the energy and the beat and I didn't know wtf I was doing but I liked it.

It had me from the off.

Then I had to learn the basics, and in short, I was the worst there. I got it, but I couldn't do it hahaha but I could see how much my Mom was killing it though, she really picked it up. The man even said "i know who's gonna be teaching who this week".

So we left, and both of us felt really good. I felt frustrated, but I was so keen to get back and start again. When I got home I was doing the steps before and after showering.

The holding the hands of your partner (UV Light exchange), looking at their face (UV Light exchange) and the movement in rhythm (resonance)... all made sense as to why I felt so good, despite Blue Light and RF wayyyyy to late on a Sunday.

this morning I didn't feel tired or fatigued, like I did on Saturday after being in BL/RF at my friends house til the same time.... this time I woke and had energy.

Above the science.... I reflected on how "tight and tense" I was inside myself and how that was being produced in my movement. It is making me conscious of my body, how I am speaking with it. I think learning to Dance could be a way to break down the Fears, lack of self-confidence, but also a way to find confidence in how to speak with both body and mind.

It also made me aware, I can be too passive with Women. it forces you to take the lead, to take control, and be the Man. El Hombre. Something, I've not done in my past.

Also, it is a Community. Everyone was smiling, engaging, you have to switch partners every couple minutes so you have to get close to strangers and show your ability... be subject to judgement in your mind, but really you get supported, encouraged, smiled at, and then ushered along at the beat of the Music... I felt a real sense of belonging. It was so much more than I expected.

Plus, me and my Mom had a good time, and broke down some more physical/ emotional barriers of our own. Together.

**Salsa is a Relationship RX, for sure.**

### Inger — 2019-10-07T13:34:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279729>

Awesome Sean!  Connecting with humans is such a big part of life! No one should miss out on that.
Connecting with no judgement. I guess it is easy to start judge others and close oneself into a a kind of "superiority"bubble when we become aware of certain things, and so we miss out of so much!
I guess that might be what Chris here on the forum recently wrote about too.

To me it is so fascinating how we can just be able to do so much differently than others around, and still be so open and connected to the world around us! It is like quantum magic to me...lol but it works!!
It does not work if we judge others or have expectations how they should be or not be though.

I have been reading the book "The human magnet syndrome", maybe your mom wants to read it. Sounds like a classical example of self-love-deficiency, and her husband sounds like a narcisisst! Those two attract each other like a strong magnet. It is so crazy.
And the cure is, self-love-abundance   There is no way you can get hurt by a narcisisst if you are self-love-abundant! (well... the narcisisst will leave you pretty fast then, to find someone else to feed on... )

### Jenelle — 2019-10-07T15:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279737>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279726)
>
> **So how did me and my Mom break down another Wall of Fear within us yesterday?
>
> SALSA.
> **
> After literally being told "I had to" learn Salsa before I hope to spend Xmas with her in Mexico, I felt pretty motivated and challenged by my girl in Canada.
>
> I have to say, she's like the perfect match for me right now... She loves anything Latin American - Salsa, Food, the People, the Art, the Culture. Plus, she speaks spanish and dances well.
>
> She even had 3 girls fly in from Costa Rica she met travelling to Montreal and stayed with her.
>
> She is pushing me. Harder and harder. But not aggressively, but leading me with her own capability. Inspiring me. Making me want to catch up, to be able to walk with her with my head held high.
>
> Something I've never, ever, ever, been comfortable with - Dancing.
>
> I'm the guy who leans against the wall and says "Salsa is for fags" while the hot dude is killing it and every woman is watching him move.
>
> It is so obviously insecurity and lack of self-confidence that it isn't even worth analysing what that is.
>
> Once again......... it is Fear.
>
> But would you expect, my own Mom to have the same Fear?
>
> I took her up on Salsa, and neither of us wanted to go last night, we knew we should but we both talked about bailing pretty much the whole way there.
>
> At the Car Park, I managed to get us past the urge to drive back and into the building.
>
> We walk in, and I see about 15 women, doing the Mambo up and down, Salsa Styling... my god, my entire mammalian reproductive male survival mode mechanism of "Abort. Abort" kicked in to avoid potential embarrassment in front of all these females.
>
> At that point, my Mom decided to take the lead. She said "come on then", even though she was dead nervous.
>
> She's very glamorous, very attractive woman. She works so hard on her appearance, yet still feels inadequate and shy. She really doesn't know her own worth. Do I? (Mirror).
>
> So we stood in the corner watching until the Beginner's class. I couldn't believe how good they looked. It was incredible.
>
> Then, we all got in and did a big dance to warm up. The music come on and I couldn't help but smile, I felt the energy and the beat and I didn't know wtf I was doing but I liked it.
>
> It had me from the off.
>
> Then I had to learn the basics, and in short, I was the worst there. I got it, but I couldn't do it hahaha but I could see how much my Mom was killing it though, she really picked it up. The man even said "i know who's gonna be teaching who this week".
>
> So we left, and both of us felt really good. I felt frustrated, but I was so keen to get back and start again. When I got home I was doing the steps before and after showering.
>
> The holding the hands of your partner (UV Light exchange), looking at their face (UV Light exchange) and the movement in rhythm (resonance)... all made sense as to why I felt so good, despite Blue Light and RF wayyyyy to late on a Sunday.
>
> this morning I didn't feel tired or fatigued, like I did on Saturday after being in BL/RF at my friends house til the same time.... this time I woke and had energy.
>
> Above the science.... I reflected on how "tight and tense" I was inside myself and how that was being produced in my movement. It is making me conscious of my body, how I am speaking with it. I think learning to Dance could be a way to break down the Fears, lack of self-confidence, but also a way to find confidence in how to speak with both body and mind.
>
> It also made me aware, I can be too passive with Women. it forces you to take the lead, to take control, and be the Man. El Hombre. Something, I've not done in my past.
>
> Also, it is a Community. Everyone was smiling, engaging, you have to switch partners every couple minutes so you have to get close to strangers and show your ability... be subject to judgement in your mind, but really you get supported, encouraged, smiled at, and then ushered along at the beat of the Music... I felt a real sense of belonging. It was so much more than I expected.
>
> Plus, me and my Mom had a good time, and broke down some more physical/ emotional barriers of our own. Together.
>
> **Salsa is a Relationship RX, for sure.**

This is one of the few times I’ve wished ~ really fucking wished ~ I had a “LOVE” button.

You just brought tears to my eyes, picturing you, your mom, all of it ... Sean ... what an incredible turnaround for you and your mom.

I will be thinking of you while I sing and dance with my kids in the kitchen. With a big smile on my face.

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-07T15:50:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279738>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=279729)
>
> Awesome Sean!  Connecting with humans is such a big part of life! No one should miss out on that.
>
> Connecting with no judgement. I guess it is easy to start judge others and close oneself into a a kind of "superiority"bubble when we become aware of certain things, and so we miss out of so much!
> I guess that might be what Chris here on the forum recently wrote about too.
>
> To me it is so fascinating how we can just be able to do so much differently than others around, and still be so open and connected to the world around us! It is like quantum magic to me...lol but it works!!
> It does not work if we judge others or have expectations how they should be or not be though.
>
> I have been reading the book "The human magnet syndrome", maybe your mom wants to read it. Sounds like a classical example of self-love-deficiency, and her husband sounds like a narcisisst! Those two attract each other like a strong magnet. It is so crazy.
> And the cure is, self-love-abundance   There is no way you can get hurt by a narcisisst if you are self-love-abundant! (well... the narcisisst will leave you pretty fast then, to find someone else to feed on... )

I've been accused, a lot, of a superiority complex lately. Sometimes it takes really going deep for that person to realise that it ain't about superiority.

But it is actually about being brave enough to take that person into their Pain.... take them to the place they fear so much. **What I must get better with... is explaining, that I'm with them, in the dark place. I don't just leave them in there.**

My Mom needs to learn her own self-worth but must travel to a place of darkness first. Because it is not him being a narcissist that is the issue, the issue is **she is allowing him her time and her energy because she is scared of the alternative.** My mom had self-love before they met, but she allowed him in because of his money. She took the comfort road. And in doing so, she paid a price.

Lately my Mom has seen how much my Rage has brought about such Clarity and magic in her life. She's scared of the truth, as we all are, but now she knows I'm doing it with her - and I'm facing my own fears - she has no excuse to not step forward.

I love your quote, in your signature Inger:
_**Courage is forward movement while embracing fear. Thinking for yourself is courage. What's rare is the courage to follow it to the dark places where it leads. One person with courage is the only majority you need to succeed in life.**_

Facing up to other people's struggles and resistance to the truth.... is like breaking a dislocated bone back into place for your friend, so they can use it again..... They don't want it to hurt, and you don't want to hurt them... but if you do not go through it, you cannot use the finger. It will die.

I'm using my Time lately much wiser than before, I'm ripping the toxic Fear out of someone that they are so desperately protecting and deflecting, even though... it will benefit them more than they know.

I've realised **_that Time is the most precious commodity for me and my family. Wasting it, by avoiding the Truth, is worse than offending someone for 5 minutes, or 1 hour, or 2 weeks.

Once that Truth is faced - people are set free._**

Nobody wants to re-locate the bone themselves, to get it back into place.

So the Doctor, or a Friend, has to break that bone back into place **for them.**

See this is the thing. I couldn't get to Salsa, or community, or meet my Girl from Canada and have this amazing relationship, or have an amazing relationship with my Mom after such despair about her, or re-build my relationship with Robin, or anyone in my life... **without going through the Uncomfortable, Hurtful Truth that was lying between us. Sometimes, you have to take a position of superiority in that situation... because the plain fact is, I am ready to go deeper and face my fears and demons... but so far, none of my Friends have been... until I took them there, and THEN THEY THANK ME.

It is actually Their Superiority Complex which is blocking them from their own true power.

Fear has controlled, and continues to control so much of our decisions...

Electric Power Grid. Central Heating. Computers. Phones. Religion. Government. The Agricultural system. Vaccines. Medicine. The Economy. Politics. Jobs. Money. Relationships. Lies. Hurt. Deceipt. Cheating.**

At the root of all of them, is driven, at some level, by our own Fear of **_not having that thing to protect us in some way.
_**
We have the same pattern or system in our way of Living our life.

I've chosen to take my Fears head on and stop them ruling my life... **what could I be, or feel, or do, if I truly did not stop myself? What power could i give to my friends, my family, if they also - did not Fear their demons?**

I know, there are still many to be broken. But I'm on the path.

Through it.... I've been able to completely change the course of my life in just a couple of Months.

I know not everyone is ready. And I have no expectations. Everyone is going at their own pace, and in their own way... But, at least, when people come to this Journal... they know that is what it is about from Page 45 onwards. If you want in on this... I'll go IN for you and tell you exactly what you need to, but don't want to hear.

You've came back to me several times now... and each time, you look to defend your position. You must know, I'll keep sounding the same Music that you probably don't want to face, but what you need to hear so you can be free.

But, deep down, I think you want to go through that pain - which is why you keep coming back for more.

If you want to PM, or Skype, I'm game if you are... But it will be the same Music you've heard already.

**_As a Friend,_** _**that is my promise and my duty.**_

### 5G Canary — 2019-10-07T15:57:45+0300

### Sean Waters — 2019-10-07T15:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-279740>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=279737)
>
> This is one of the few times I’ve wished ~ really fucking wished ~ I had a “LOVE” button.
>
> You just brought tears to my eyes, picturing you, your mom, all of it ... Sean ... what an incredible turnaround for you and your mom.
>
> I will be thinking of you while I sing and dance with my kids in the kitchen. With a big smile on my face.

Thank you!

It is remarkable. I'm glad you love it and you can take some wisdom from it...

We danced together last night and for the first time neither of us felt uncomfortable with each other at proximity.

Of course, we both felt very uncomfortable with how awful our rhythm was - but that can be learned - **we've gone past the point of discomfort now, and it is only up from here.

We are totally Free, in each other's company... no Lies, no tension, no avoidance. Just Truth & Honesty, and having each other's backs.**

Today I actually started to see her as one of my friends again.

**Awesome Sean, this ^^ is exactly what I mean when I say.. to stop.. and face the own pain to heal.**

still, in the end we are alone in taking that step into freedom...

There is not much sexier than a man who can dance, and especially LEAD. (Though I must say the fact that your rhythm with mom is a bit off is probably a good thing. )
