>
> 20\. Meditate, thank God for the opportunity and take it easy...... in 6 weeks I will re-test Organic Acids Test

This sounds like a real nice protocol. Dr William Davis' book Super Gut has been a game changer for my health this past month. When I was really sick with CD I battled CDiff over a couple years. I did tons and tons of flagyl and vancomycin over those years, which probably has done insane amounts of damage to my gut, the vanco worked on and off. I failed two fecal transplants via colposcopy, it wasnt until the third oral transplant that my CD was put back into remission and Cdiff was put into a lax state. It was crazy how well it worked, all the biologics, steroids, methotrexate, didnt do shit, then this transplant puts me in remission for 3 years?? Fast forward to now, I have been following the super gut protocol trying to fix my sibo and food allergies. After adding in carbs this summer, my gut would get bloated and I would feel like shit (exactly how you were describing it sean). After doing some research about sibo I had no idea if I had it or not, then Greenfield had on Dr Davis (I had been waiting for this episode) and after that pod I was ready to start his protocol. I got his book and for the past month have been doing everything.

1\. The sibo yoghurt: Fermenting three strains together for 36 hours:Lactobacillus Gasseri, Lactobacillus Reuteri, Bacillus Coagulans. These strains inhabit the small bowel (important for me since thats all i have) and push the fecal bacteria that ascend from the large bowel (like cdiff, ecoli, etc) out. They produce bacteriocins, or natural peptide antibiotics, that kill off the sibo bacteria. I eat this daily 1/2 cups and wil be doing this for as long as its not a PITA. I have already noticed a toleration of new foods such as organic tomatoes from our garden, cantaloupe, wild berries, and some rice. I also have severely curbed the intense bloat I would get following meals.

2\. Fermenting Saccharomyces boulardii in juice for 48 hours: This is another thing Dr Davis recommends for SIFO. You empty one capsule of S Boul into a bottle of organic 100 juice and ferment it for 48 hours. It eats more than half the sugar content of the juice and you get a wild amount of S boul from the ferment. Only concern would be the d2, but i notice it hasn't affected me, esp since your not consuming that much per day.

3\. Turmeric and herbs: Davis makes the point that we dont want to maximize absorption for the turmeric as we want it staying in the gut fighting off invaders.

4\. Clove and green tea: I have been doing this to help restore the mucosal lining to my gut. Davis talks about how fecal bacteria ravage the lining of your get which separates us from the bacteria. When the lining is gone and the bacteria die off they leave behind their cell walls which contain LPS which causes endotoxemia. This I think is a major part of IBD and other auto immune. Sean once explained to me how body builders get dysbiosis which then leads to CD or UC and at the time I didn't understand why, but now it makes perfect sense. The wearing of the mucosal lining leads to endotoxemia which then leads to high amounts of inflammation in the blood (from the crazy amount of [Lipopolysaccharide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipopolysaccharide) in the blood) which then leads to your immune system going bonkers. Wild stuff.

5\. Lots of colostrum: Utilizing the amazing gut benefits of colostrum like lactoferrin for mucosal lining has been another big game changer. I have been using a grass fed goat colostrum. I have been tryin to get it from the local farms in frozen form but everybody is out for the season.

6\. Digestive enzymes: I have been using Health Gut holozyme enzymes (Sara from the carnivore yogi pod has on Steve wright the founder all the time so I wanted to try em out). These have helped with digestion in general and I think are worth continuing, they are a bit pricey so I may try Mass enzymes next.

7\. Bone broth: making bone broth from 100 GF beef bones

8\. Starting l glutamine today

I am also starting to eat onions and garlic per the same reasons.

I feel like my gut is performing really well under this protocol, I really cant recommend the book enough.

It eats more than half the sugar content of the juice and you get a wild amount of S boul from the ferment. Only concern would be the d2, but i notice it hasn't affected me, esp since your not consuming that much per day.
>
> 3\. Turmeric and herbs: Davis makes the point that we dont want to maximize absorption for the turmeric as we want it staying in the gut fighting off invaders.
>
> 4\. Clove and green tea: I have been doing this to help restore the mucosal lining to my gut. Davis talks about how fecal bacteria ravage the lining of your get which separates us from the bacteria. When the lining is gone and the bacteria die off they leave behind their cell walls which contain LPS which causes endotoxemia. This I think is a major part of IBD and other auto immune. Sean once explained to me how body builders get dysbiosis which then leads to CD or UC and at the time I didn't understand why, but now it makes perfect sense. The wearing of the mucosal lining leads to endotoxemia which then leads to high amounts of inflammation in the blood (from the crazy amount of [Lipopolysaccharide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipopolysaccharide) in the blood) which then leads to your immune system going bonkers. Wild stuff.
>
> 5\. Lots of colostrum: Utilizing the amazing gut benefits of colostrum like lactoferrin for mucosal lining has been another big game changer. I have been using a grass fed goat colostrum. I have been tryin to get it from the local farms in frozen form but everybody is out for the season.
>
> 6\. Digestive enzymes: I have been using Health Gut holozyme enzymes (Sara from the carnivore yogi pod has on Steve wright the founder all the time so I wanted to try em out). These have helped with digestion in general and I think are worth continuing, they are a bit pricey so I may try Mass enzymes next.
>
> 7\. Bone broth: making bone broth from 100 GF beef bones
>
> 8\. Starting l glutamine today
>
> I am also starting to eat onions and garlic per the same reasons.
>
> I feel like my gut is performing really well under this protocol, I really cant recommend the book enough.

Scotty... can you share the recipe of the Yoghurt in full please for us all here?

As well as the Juice one... Thank you my brother.

I will try out all of the above including the podcasts and the book.

### Scott klein — 2022-09-14T17:39:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-315674>

Sorry Sean just seeing this, not on here but once a week or so!

Juice recipe: Take one Saccharomyces boulardii capsule containing 500mg of the yeast and add to a 32 fluid oz or 1 litre bottle of juice. Its recommended to use a high quality organic juice with nothing other than the juice itself. After you pop the capsule open into the juice, swirl around the bottle so the yeast can get a nice mixing, then let it sit for 48hrs. The S. boul will eat majority of the sugar content (I would also assume the d2 idk) and make a sparkling juice. I typically drink less than a 1/2 cup a day.

Sibo Yoghurt: Take ten BioGia Gastrus tablets aka L Reutri crush them into a powder, 1 Capsule of Gasseri opened, 1 Capsule of B. Coagulans opened and 2 tablespoons of prebiotic fiber like inulin or potato starch (this is what the bacteria feed on during the ferment), mix these together in a little bowl with the milk you are using for the yoghurt, just enough so its dissolved. Once you have that done put them in your fermenting device (I use an instapot, find one with a adjustable setting) with your milk and set for 106F for 36 hours. Once you have a finished batch instead of using the capsules and tablets you will just seed the next batch with 2tbsp of the prior batch.

A couple of things to take note of when fermenting the yogurt: 1. The L reutri dies at 108F and perfers to ferment at human body temp, the Gasseri likes 109, and B Coagulans likes 119F, Davis finds a happy medium for them to co ferment at 106, HOWEVER, I think fermenting the L Reutri separate at 99F/100F generates a high count, likewise with the Gasseri and Coagulans combined at 109. 2. Because my instapot overcompensates on heat up, I dont add the bacertia mix until it is past the preheat/warmup. So I will typically heat the milk alone in the instapot at 180F (makes the consitency better) for 20 min, then after the 20 min I set the temp to whatever strain I am doing that day (so in the above case either 100 for L Reutri or 109 for gasseri and Coagulans) and wait for the milk to reach that temp. Usually the instapot will start the timer once the temp is met. If you dont do it this way you might kill off the starter mix while the instapot preheats and overcompensates. Keep in mind this is a 36 hour process and can be a PITA always making separate yoghurts so if you wanna do it at 106 that is fine and what is suggested I just like to go overboard on this stuff. Your yoghurt will be pretty acidic and may have a light carbonation. Make sure you dont f with the yoghurt while its fermenting.

If your interested in the herbal antibiotic treatment I am starting that as well here soon and will keep the thread posted on protocol specs.

Last thing, Davis talks a lot about how ineffective 99% of commercial probiotics are and how most are scams for a factor of reasons. Typically the mixes are totally random and have no benefit at all, your not getting high enough counts, they die off really fast and dont inhabit the Gi tract. The diff with this method and using these strains is pretty important... 1. These are keystone species and encourage the growth of other important lost microbes 2. They inhabit the small bowel (where sibo occurs) and release natural antibiotics that kill off sibo bacteria 3. When fermented in the yoghurt you get very high counts. Now they still do not take up residence in the GI tract permanently, but if you eat it frequently your still get benefits and overtime your biome will improve.

S Boul: [https://www.amazon.com/Florastor-Da...astor&qid=1663163990&s=hpc&sr=1-2-spons&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/Florastor-Daily-Probiotic-Supplement-Women/dp/B01NB0G1V8/ref=sr_1_2_sspa?keywords=florastor&qid=1663163990&s=hpc&sr=1-2-spons&psc=1)
L Reutri: [https://www.amazon.com/BioGaia-Supp...&s=hpc&sr=1-1-spons&psc=1&smid=A1ORNN1SHDDNYS](https://www.amazon.com/BioGaia-Supplement-Discomfort-Constipation-Regularity/dp/B01AH3RT9Y/ref=sr_1_1_sspa?keywords=biogaia&qid=1663164482&s=hpc&sr=1-1-spons&psc=1&smid=A1ORNN1SHDDNYS)
Gasseri: [https://www.amazon.com/Dr-Mercola-Probiotic-Supplement-Regularity/dp/B09WFVP7BF/ref=sr\_1\_4?crid=3SNQ2E4N45HPU&keywords=mercola+gasseri&qid=1663164534&s=hpc&sprefix=mercole+gasseri+,hpc,105&sr=1-4](https://www.amazon.com/Dr-Mercola-Probiotic-Supplement-Regularity/dp/B09WFVP7BF/ref=sr_1_4?crid=3SNQ2E4N45HPU&keywords=mercola+gasseri&qid=1663164534&s=hpc&sprefix=mercole+gasseri+%2Chpc%2C105&sr=1-4)
B Coagulans:[https://www.amazon.com/Digestive-Advantage-Daily-Probiotic-Capsules/dp/B01ND16K2I/ref=sr\_1\_2\_sspa?crid=HRGCOZA0BVUZ&keywords=schiff+digestive+advantage&qid=1663164610&rdc=1&s=hpc&sprefix=schiff+digestive+advangtage,hpc,107&sr=1-2-spons&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/Digestive-Advantage-Daily-Probiotic-Capsules/dp/B01ND16K2I/ref=sr_1_2_sspa?crid=HRGCOZA0BVUZ&keywords=schiff+digestive+advantage&qid=1663164610&rdc=1&s=hpc&sprefix=schiff+digestive+advangtage%2Chpc%2C107&sr=1-2-spons&psc=1)
Instapot: [https://www.amazon.com/Instant-Pot-...663167158&sprefix=instant+pot+,aps,156&sr=8-3](https://www.amazon.com/Instant-Pot-Plus-60-Programmable/dp/B01NBKTPTS/ref=sr_1_3?crid=1HDGEXZ7UEHK2&keywords=instant+pot&qid=1663167158&sprefix=instant+pot+%2Caps%2C156&sr=8-3)

Here are a couple more resources-

[https://drdavisinfinitehealth.com/2022/03/sibo-yogurt/](https://drdavisinfinitehealth.com/2022/03/sibo-yogurt/)
_"We co-ferment for 36 hours, then consume 1/2-cup per day for 4 weeks. I didn’t know what to expect at first but, so far, this mixture has performed way beyond my expectations. Of around 30 people who have tried “SIBO Yogurt,” 90% have normalized breath H2 and obtained relief from SIBO symptoms. Given that the best in conventional healthcare is rifaximin with a track record of effectiveness of around 50%, if the 90% with the SIBO Yogurt holds up, that would be spectacular. The experience is preliminary and anecdotal, but promising."_
_

### Sean Waters — 2022-09-15T23:28:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-315737>

Update.

I'm only 3 weeks into my protocol.......... here's what has happened:

1\. I camped at 4000m elevation in 0° Celsius at the site of an exploded Volcano in Mexico - ate the Rice dish (Paella) made by the Hiking guides which would usually fuck me up - and then proceeded to awaken first at 4.30am - complete an 8km at seriously high altitude (25% less oxygen) down into the basin at 3800m and then summit the 4500m ridge, only to turn around and do it all in reverse... all while Fasted with no prior altitude experience.

Notice the pic below, _**I'm the only one not wearing trousers, thermals or boots @ 3° celsius
**_
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

By about 10pm _**I was shivering quite hard ...**_ I realised I wasn't quite as "Wim Hof" as I thought LOL... So I put on my Boots, Socks and a Jacket... others were layered and layers... I slept in the same and felt warm all night... but, my sleep struggled with the altitude and the rice didn't help... but by about 4.30am I was still the first one up and about, Circadian rhythms on point... and I skipped breakfast and we began our ascent....... The Alitude was the highest and hardest I've ever experienced. Prior to this, I'd only ever done mountains at 1000m (Snowdonia) and some skiing at 2000 to 2500m in Tahoe, but not much else.

This dog accompanied us ALL NIGHT and ALL DAY on the hike... he was a stray, but he lived on the mountain... he was _**completely cold adapted.**_ He put all of us to shame to be honest.

This is the summit 1 of 2, where they had put a Cross, which the Catholics usually do for every peak in every area I've seen.

### Sean Waters — 2022-09-15T23:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-315741>

Alright so continuing on....

2\. I have been consistent with the whole protocol and eating _**Liberal amounts of Fermented Foods... kombucha/ kefir/ yoghurt/ honey garlic/ pickles an sauerkraut
**_
I noticed that the Kombucha products from the store are High Sugar!! and although they impart a good effect nonetheless, making them myself I think is the key to improving that.

All my vital signs are good - as stated, I hiked up at 4000-4500m an absolutely killed it. I had almost zero DOMS (muscle soreness) the next day.

I was able to spend 2 nights in Mexico City swimming in EMF near a cell tower and 20 million people... I felt pretty awesome there actually.

I've been able to Eat Out more than ever before... tolerating all kinds of shit like Keto Brownies and Oxalate foods and Spices/ Bad oils .. you name it.

My entire physiology seems **ROBUST.

I've not had such freedom and liberation from suffering due to food in about 6 years... the last time I felt this good and clear was 2016, which was when I was given a similar herbal anti-bacterial protocol.
**
Unfortunately, the Naturopath never actually told me what the issue was - instead choosing to throw me the products and move to the next client. It worked... but I never knew _**Why.
**_
If she had indulged in me though, I would probably never have found Circadian Biology, the Sun, Mexico.. all the things I've gained from taking this laborious and ultra-tough road to healing myself.... I have done everything the long way, and guess what - it's taught me the absolute essentials of Life with a level of experience and perspective that no book or theory could possibly offer.

I've used this pain, and transcended it, I've morphed it into my own power and gains to make my life fucking incredible.

Although I was doing so much better in the tropics... still, my rash returned quite severely and my issues returned badly after a 4 month bio-hack on resistant starch (Nuts, Almonds etc.)... I obviously loaded myself with Glyphosate (non organic Almonds) and destroyed my Gut Flora... a note on the January 2014 webinar - make sure they're Organic before you carry that one out.

I begged God in June 2022 this year to show me the way.... Please, just show me the way... I'm done with this lesson, whatever it takes, show me the way.

When you ask, he will provide.

If I hadn't have gone on this journey, I would have never have met my beautiful girlfriend in June 2019, the one who caused such an earth shattering introduction to the influence of the Universe, God, Source.. whatever you want to call it....

I would have never have met Jack... never moved to Mexico... so many things had to happen through this process of delusional sufferring.

All along... the answer was right there waiting for me.

It's funny though, I tried to order the same Naturopathic protocol from 2016 several times in Mexico and for some strange reason... the orders got blocked or failed somehow, shipping issues.... _**Even when I wanted to heal... God made me wait, in order for me to find Kriya Yoga... Paramahansa... he needed me to suffer a little more, for my own Spiritual growth.
**_
Ultimately, that is the purpose of this, for me anyway... I see no other reason why I was taken on this journey other than Liberation.

I'm not done yet, not even close. But, I've been equipped with so much knowledge, so many gifts, I've got the Quantum Biological toolkit of the genius of Jack Kruse, combined with the spiritual prowess and wisdom of the likes of Lahiri Mahasaya, Sri Yukteswar and Paramahansa yogananda... I've got residency in Mexico... I'm secured with Bitcoin... I have my girl of my dreams back in my life, living with me full time, remote, and free to go wherever we please... I've got my health back and all of these incredible friends and people I've met along the way.

Thank you x 10,000,000 to every single bit of suffering I've been gifted.

### Sean Waters — 2022-09-15T23:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-315743>

**Transcend Suffering. Attain Bliss.**

Nothing hits harder for me right now than 12+UVI at elevation........HOME.

### Sean Waters — 2022-09-20T05:06:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-315875>

I **came a long way to get where I am today....... [www.thebodyclock.co](http://www.thebodyclock.co/) is my new website and the start of my Official journey as a Health Practitioner. Although, I have been helping many people on a more informal basis for the last 5 years.**

He looks to bring the best out of you. To raise you up. In order to do that, he must feel his best himself.

Please forgive any faux pauz I make here, I don't do the social media thing often. But after much reading here I understand this is a great group of like-minded people. It's been a long journey and I'll try to keep it concise. I recently had labs done and I could use some help with the interpreting. The labs are the only ones I could get my doctor to order, here in New York State the places to get your own lab work done are unavailable to us. Been looking for a work-around for awhile and haven't found one yet. So here goes with the brief history and recent labs:

Female
53 yrs old
NOT menopausal
blood pressure issues for 25yrs, some relief with daily high doses of Magnesium
currently taking 50mg Losartan (only prescript)
Current vitamins are: 5000IU D3, Multi B, bitter-melon, 800mg Magnesium + lotion
Lost 23lbs and still going
diet is Keto/Epi-paleo since Sept 2017
I use blue-blockers sunset-sunrise, flux on computer and cellphone
I live in upstate NY, lat 43
I am APOE 3/4
Labs on 1/15/18:
HA1C 5.7 (8/17 test was 6.2)
fasting BG 124
Total Chol 259 (8/17 test was 228)
HDL 55 (8/17 test was 42)
Chol/HDL ratio 4.7 98/17 test was 5.4)
Trig 163 (8/17 test was 609)
Bun 14
Creatinine .94
BUN/Creat 15
Sodium 139
Potassium 4.4
Chloride 104
CO2 25
Anion Gap 10
Calcium 9.4
Total Protein 6.6
Albumin 4.4
Globulin 2.2
A/G Ratio 2.0
Alk Phos 45
AST 22
ALT 21
Bili, Total 0.5
IRON 38
TIBC 438
Ferritin 7
Transferrin 361
TSH 2.53
Vit D 25 HYDROXY 26
Vit B12 554

I was told by the nurse the doctor will be calling me today to discuss my Iron supp options and VitD3 as she is concerned my D3 was low even though I currently take 5000IU daily. I had planned to add CT to my protocol after testing so I had a baseline for learning its benefits, but not sure I should add it with low D3 and anemia for second time.

Also not completely sure how to participate in this great community. I see there have been some changes, don't know exactly how to access the new blog posts by Jack etc. Any guidance there would be welcomed. I do follow him on both FB and Instagram as well as this website, but seem to missing something as I have come to this party more recently....Extremely happy to be here though, finally!!!!!

>
> Spend lots of time outdoor totally naked and without any sunscreen or eyeglasses.

I agree with that especially if you can be naked at a lower latitude in winter for any amount of time.

FWIW the first time I remember using an automated BP gauge was at my grandma's house. I was 14 and my BP was 140/85. Up to about a year ago it pretty much stayed the same. Sunlight and CT later and I'm regularly 120/70. That's a heck of a lot less beating on my vascular system.

I went 3 decades without understanding the mechanism. My mom went at least 5 decades and suffered a stroke along the way. Just yesterday ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/after-9-months.20448/page-3#post-233276](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/after-9-months.20448/page-3#post-233276) ) I stopped jibber-jabbering about all things science, slapped a BP cuff on her RIGHT after **nap** (141/95) then put her in the sun while I chilled in her 49 F pool. 30 minutes of sun on her and she was 128/68. She was positively stunned and the results alone spoke volumes to her. She requested me to help her repeat the same thing today and I did. Family seem to suck at listening to each other, but hopefully this made a dent.

Settle in and absorb what you can here. I think it'll make a significant impact on your quality of life.

# Thread: Journal of autonomic dysfunction, including studies and attempts to correct
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-autonomic-dysfunction-including-studies-and-attempts-to-correct.20762/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael CULLEN — 2018-01-24T19:56:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-autonomic-dysfunction-including-studies-and-attempts-to-correct.20762/post-233380>

Hi everyone

I live in the UK and have had health issues for half my life. I was never sick as a kid. Nobody in my family has any genetic diseases that we know of.

I have had 23andme testing done. Can post results later.

The last decade I have been back and forth with the NHS, but they are useless.

Here are my autonomic studies results.

Cardiovascular reflexes: Resting cardiac vagal tone (CVT): was 2.64 units in the linear vagal scale (LVS) which is an abnormally very low resting cardiac vagal tone, (Normal range, 5-10 units in the LVS), associated with no Abnormal Spontaneous Brainstem Activation (ASBAs). There was very low spontaneous baroreflex function. Resting heart rate: was 91 beats/min, which is a resting tachycardia consistent with this level of CVT. Breathing: there was normal breathing at the rate of 17 breaths/min. Deep breathing: CVT was 4.88 units and the maximum CVT was 8.29 units in the LVS indicating normal respiratory modulation of CVT within the brainstem during deep breathing. Carotid massage: CVT increased by 3.1 units in the LVS showing a low cardiodepressor effect (normal increase 5-20 units), blood pressure (BP) changed by -16.0 mmHg indicating a normal vasodepressor effect (normal drop 10-25 mmHg). Baroreflex responsiveness in isometric exercise: was 4.09 ms/mmHg and 5.99 ms/mmHg was predicted from the patient’s height, it indicates a normal central gain of the baroreflex system. Valsalva’s ratio: was normal at 1.47 (normal range, 1.2-1.8).

Nutritive Peripheral Circulation: Supine pO2 was 69.0 (should normally be above 60 mmHg) and supine pCO2 was 41.2 mmHg (normal range; 39-44 mmHg). There was good nutritive gaseous exchange in peripheral tissues at rest. There were also good gaseous responses during deep breathing indicating normal diffusion pathway into peripheral tissues.
Orthostasis: Cardiac response: showed a normal response in a 30:15 ratio test. BP stability: was poor, systolic BP varied by -70.0 mmHg, normal variation is <25 mmHg. Mean supine arterial BP was 92.3 mmHg indicating normal supine pressure (the normal range of supine mean arterial BP, 80-105 mmHg). Orthostatic hypotension: Postural change in diastolic BP was 18.4 mmHg, so no postural hypotension. There was good inotropic response on standing upright.

Sympathetic function in general: There was no test done for postganglionic damage. The BP evidence suggests a normal baseline supine sympathetic tone. There was poor baseline inotropic function. Control of resistance blood vessels in skeletal muscles during isometric exercise: showed muscle sympathetic failure. There was inotropic fatigue on sitting upright. Cardioaccelerator function in isometric exercise: showed a cardioaccelerator failure. There was normal inotropic response to isometric exercise. Blood pressure response to Valsalva’s manoeuvre: BP change in phase IIe was -38.8 mmHg and in phase III was -6.8 mmHg showing evidence of reduced venous return. BP change in phase IIi was 11.2 mmHg indicating a normal splanchnic sympathetic tone.

Sudomotor function in the skin
Vasomotor failure in the skin
Emotional sudomotor function was not asessed
Thermoregulatory vasomotor failure was detected in all 4 limbs

Interpretation: The results show evidence of muscle sympathetic failure, cardioaccelerator failure but a normal splanchnic sympathetic tone in the deep target organs of the sympathetic division of the autonomic nervous system. There was evidence of reduced venous return to the heart. In the cutaneous targets, there was generalised thermoregulatory vasomotor failure. In the parasympathetic division, there was abnormally very low resting cardiac vagal tone. Baroreflex system had a normal central gain and there was normal respiratory modulation of the CVT within the brainstem during deep breathing. There was low cardiodepressor but normal vasodepressor effects of the carotid reflex. Of the non-specific tests, there was no postural hypotension, a normal Valsalva's ratio and normal response of the heart to standing upright.

Conclusion and Recommendations: This patient has sympathetic dysfunctions in the heart and in skeletal muscles and a very low central parasympathetic activity associated with a skewed baroreflex dysfunction in which only the cardiodepressor function of the carotid is affected. The pattern of central parasympathetic disturbances suggests that this patient should be investigated for possible exposure to environmental toxins, particularly pesticides. He currently has good nutritive gaseous exchange in peripheral tissues at rest and the good gaseous responses in peripheral tissues during deep breathing indicate good diffusion pathway into tissues. The patient has inotropic fatigue during orthostasis and when sitting upright, which means he will have exercise intolerance and will fatigue easily. He therefore requires support of the inotropic function of the heart with dietary supplements.

Dr. Peter Julu, MBChB, MSc, PhD
Specialist Autonomic Neurophysiologist and Consultant Physician

This was a few years ago, and now I am dealing with bursts of adrenaline on top of this. Mainly in the middle of the night.

**Steps I have taken since discovering Dr Kruse:**
I am drinking Icelandic Glacier water, 1.5- 2 litres per day
I only watch TV, or go on laptop with blue blockers on
My laptop has wifi turned off and is connected via ethernet
I use low radiation router, with wifi on BT router turned off. It is also programmed to go off at night. I have measured this with EMF meter and there is no radiation when before set time.
I use truedark glasses at night, 2-3 hours before going to bed.

I eat only real foods, mainly Organic. High fat and protein with some carbs in nuts. I just had an Organic rib eye, cooked in pure beef fat.

I was getting out first thing in the morning for AM light, but am struggling to cope with cold right now. My tolerance has drastically dropped. I can not stand for long periods, and getting in and out of chairs is difficult. My father helped me with that, as I used to sit in back yard(tested and close to no nnemf there). Now he is gone it is immensely problematic to actually get out of the house.

The main issue I am dealing with now is excessive oxygen consumption, even at rest. Mitochondrial studies done show this. I awake with adrenaline and sometimes out of breath. My lung function is fine, but I had issues after test. I am very fatigued.

I am definitely in fight or flight mode most of the time. When my father died my heart was racing for 36 hours straight.

The last tests I did were hair analysis and Fecal metals analysis. BOTH showed I am dumping high amounts of nickel and mercury. I did not take any chelators. I am not deliberately trying to detox.

where to start, hey?

God bless

Consider buying a quartz sounding bowl and learning to use it to use sound to rebuild your EZ inside your cells.
5.  1.  Cold water face immersion after exercise also stimulates the vagus and the mammalian dive reflex. I spoke about this in the CT series. Filling the mouth with cold ice water or cooled saliva and submerging your tongue to trigger a hyper-relaxing vagal response. If you really want to push it putting crushed ice in your mouth with cold water really stimulates the ramus lingualis of the vagus nerve in the mouth. Stimulating the vagus nerve naturally with light and breath with these hacks above will far outperform any other maneuver. They are all easy to do and free to try. I’ve taught many members this during live events.
    2.  When you really improve your condition, you might even consider learning free diving breathing. This is an advanced form of vagal stimulation. I’ve used this for bio-hacks in cystic fibrosis and and multiple sclerosis successfully.
    3.  Artificial vagal stimulation is possible with a vagal nerve stimulator, but there are no indications for use with cancer at this time. I have a sense this might change with time. The most interesting link for cancer patients is the depression link. Depression and cancer are tightly coupled. Depression is always associated with a stress response and a low DC electric current in tissues. Low Vitamin D3 levels are an ideal proxy for depression and cancer links. Depression and cancer diagnosis are often linked in life and in the literature. Your doctor may not make this link for you but you must.
    4.  _Vagal stimulation improves all of the following as shown in a pub med search:_ It turns on and amplifies neurogenesis, helping our brains sprout new brain cells. Rapidly turn off the stress, hyper-arousal, and fight/flight via the relaxation response. Sharpen our memories. Fight inflammatory disease. Help you resist high blood pressure. Block the hormone cortisol and other oxidizing agents that age and deteriorate the brain and body Block systemic (body-wide) inflammation which is a major factor behind aging and poor health. Help us overcome depression and anxiety. Help us sleep better to regenerate the immune system to fight the cancer as nature has built within us. Raise levels of human growth hormone. You will often here growth hormone is a bad thing for cancers. Exogenous growth hormone is but not the growth hormone from your own pituitary gland. It help us overcome insulin resistance and natural sunlight gives this the best massive boost. Dealing with insulin resistance with food is a futile battle if you are not getting full spectrum sunlight via your retina daily. Vagal stimulation can turns down allergic responses and immune response that overshoot because of poor signaling due to inflammation. Inflammation = protons and pH is a measure of proton gradient. The lower the pH the lower the thickness and power contained within your exclusion zone of cell water. This lowers chances of getting stress, seizures, and tension/migraine headaches. Help spare and grow our mitochondria by stimulating autophagy and avoiding apoptosis; this is a key to maintaining optimal energy levels and not causing massive over expression of our DNA and RNA machinery that occurs in cancer states. It gives you more time to affect the environmental changes mentioned above. Affect our overall ability to live longer, healthier, and more energetic lives.
    5.  Use of isolation tanks in heavy water with a combination of artificial UV and IR lights within the tub or its environment is a consideration. Humming and listening to running water in this state markedly stimulate several senses that the vagus nerve monitors. I have used some optical hacks with light via my eyes to augment these effects. I will be speaking about them in the June 2016 webinar. This might be hard to pull off in your home, unless you build your own as I have. You can simulate it in a spa or hot tub.
    6.  Exercise to stimulate deep breathing. Breathing stimulates cooling because of the effect of CO2 over our lung surface. This can vary depending upon the person’s condition. The exercise must be done outside with as little as clothing on as possible.
    7.  Avoid sunglasses at all time. If you wear contacts remove them and go back to glasses. When outdoors take your glasses off. When indoors make sure you are blocking blue frequencies 400- 465 nm with blue tech lenses or some equivalent. _**You vagus nerve rami comes to the surface of your orbit around your eye muscles and blocking frequencies of light can and will alter the size and shape of your eye to alters vagal tone. This is controlled by the radix oculomotoria. Just stimulating your eyes with rubbing them can sensitize your vagus nerve.**_
    8.  15 minutes of sounds of falling water or crashing waves, waterfalls, or splashing rivers will give you a 20-30% drop in stress hormones (cortisol and epinephrine) This can stimulate the vagal rami in the ear and ear canal. You don’t always need to meditate to take advantage of this because water alone can meditates you. The vagus nerve and water are intimately tied to all of our senses in ways that are quantized to the dissolved gases in water to make vibrations. Those vibrations can positively affect mitochondrial resonance. Dr. Doug Wallace has found a key frequency for optimized mitochondrial function and the geometry of the respiratory proteins on the cristae. Soundscape ecology helps soothe the PVN and stimulate the vagus nerve because our sound processing systems were built to be attuned to the smallest sounds. This phonon tuning began in the womb when we were surrounded by water. Today’s urbanized noise is not just annoying, it has been found to be lethal because it can generate solitons on cell membranes that alter vagal tone to our detriment.
    9.  Looking of pictures of the ocean daily can also have the same effect because it alters our mood according to cognitive neuroscientist Petr Janata from Univ. of California at Davis.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-28T21:22:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-autonomic-dysfunction-including-studies-and-attempts-to-correct.20762/post-233694>

There is another link to poor redox to consider too: Is human autonomic dysfunction tied to environmental factors we discount in healthcare? It turns out recents studies in China, where this disease is skyrocketing has linked pollution and geo-engineering to the condition. Urban air pollution is associated with inflammation, oxidative stress, blood coagulation and autonomic dysfunction si- multaneously in healthy young humans, with sulfate and O3 as two major traffic-related pollutants contributing to such effects. Here is the paper: The Effect of Urban Air Pollution on Inflammation, Oxidative Stress, Coagulation, and Autonomic Dysfunction in Young Adults [https://www.atsjournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1164/rccm.200611-1627OC](https://www.atsjournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1164/rccm.200611-1627OC)

The increase in
8-OHdG,
fibrinogen,
and PAI-1, and the reduction in HRV remained significantly associ-ated with 3-day averaged sulfate and O3 levels in two-pollutant models. There were moderate correlations (r-0.3) between blood markers of hs-CRP, fibrinogen, PAI-1, and HRV indices.
\============
Steady-state levels of DNA damages represent the balance between formation and repair. Swenberg et al.[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/8-Oxo-2%27-deoxyguanosine#cite_note-3) measured average frequencies of steady state endogenous DNA damages in mammalian cells. The most frequent oxidative DNA damage normally present in DNA is **8-oxo-dG**, occurring at an average frequency of 2,400 per cell.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) [@Michael CULLEN](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20925/)

Some of these suggestions are confusing to me, especially the one surrounding stimulating the vagus nerve. Many people with autonomic dysfunction, like myself, have hyper vagal tone. My body is in a chronic state of excess parasympathetic activation and sympathetic withdrawal. I faint when I stand due to vasovagal syncope, and stimulating my vagus nerve makes me worse. A bit of ice on my neck helps when I am overheating, or taking a semi cold shower, due to my temperature dysregulation. But, when I attempted to try the cold thermogenesis and my autonomic dysfunction was at bay (I was standing all day comfortably) sticking my face in a bowl of ice made me feel like I was going to pass out with an instant vasovagal episode. For me, calming my vagus nerve is actually what helps. Or finding a happy medium/balance. With Midodrine and Nortriptyline I was able to keep my pressure up and use the nortriptyline (10mg) as an anticholinergic that blocks the action of acetylcholine. Salt helps a lot, so I use Haine's sea salt with added iodine. Nystatin has helped me even more.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705282/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705282/)
"In a study published by Joseph Brewer, MD, and colleagues in 2013, 93 percent of a group of 112 patients with chronic fatigue syndrome were noted to have elevated mold toxins in their urine."

How does one control these autonomic dysfunctions in a 5G world?

# Thread: Jon's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jonathin — 2018-01-25T02:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-233413>

After over 5 years of following Jack and the others on this forum, I decided to start this journal. The motivation is that despite improvement in health and living over that time I've have a concern that reared its head in just the last few months. I have been having a pressure in my upper abdomen. This is weird as I am a skinny guy of 62 yr and never had gut problems that I was aware of. This was mainly below the diaphragm, mainly on the right side. Thinking all sorts of bad thoughts I went and got a blood panel and the biggest issue was a ferritin level of 547 \[30-400 range\]. I have ferritin labs from 2004-2012 that progressed from 97 to 386. I also have a tranferrin test in 2012 of 277 \[188-341 range\].

There are other areas for me that have been off for the last 6 yrs:
BUN/creatinine stays at 18 =-
somewhat high TSH but T3 ok but not great
high sex hormone binding globulin
glucose around 100 +- (in winter eating low carb! )
everything else is relatively normal over the years

So it is looking like iron overload is the issue with liver swelling and some of the other symptoms mentioned on the web. I have not gone to a doctor yet but it looks like blood letting is the main strategy to get levels down to reduce inflammation, cancer risk, etc. Short term I can do that but long term becomes the issue. Obviously, giving up blood is using marrow stem cells. There are certainly other tests such as 23&me, etc.

I am looking for some thoughts about this issue. Yes, I can tighten up some of my lifestyle but I pretty closely follow the optimal protocols. I do live rurally in W. Colorado so the native environment is not manmade poor but at a high altitude of 5500 ft and 39 degrees latitude this may be a stress. Fortunately, the sun shines a lot especially this year. 10 yrs ago I moved from a warmer climate in AZ.

I have a concern about cooking especially meat in cast iron. The pans are very well seasoned and used at low temperature but still it could be significant source of bad forms of iron. Right now we are trying not to use them. Unfortunately, oysters and clams are higher than beef in iron. I don't eat them all the time so this might not be a big concern.

There is another significant body issue which is from 30 yrs of massive fruit eating. Blood sugar flux and carb cravings are long gone but it seems to have left me with slight neuropathy in the extremities (touch sensation is somewhat diminished) and somewhat eroded teeth which are stable now and gums are good.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-25T02:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-233415>

Meat, muscle meat should be eat close to raw avoiding cast iron.

Sun, Lugols & lots of salt.

...........

### Jonathin — 2018-01-26T00:20:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-233497>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
I get lots of sun considering it is winter. I have heard a mention somewhere about Lugols for excess iron but no explanation. In the past I did lots of potassium iodine and still have some. I go back and forth with increasing salt in general. It works will with slabs of gf beef fat this time of year. I saw the thread on salt recently and in the distant past my blood pressure was definitely on the low side. I don't check bp as this point.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I appreciate raw meat but do not eat it that way all the time. Certainly it is usually rare.

The hack seems to be to turn iron levels around. This is very measurable so I am thinking some phlebotomies, green tea and milk thistle might be a strategy as well as tightening up some of the optimal protocols. I am trying to get insurance straightened out so I can do the blood letting end of it. I would like to do as little as necessary to get the situation turned around in the right direction.

### Jonathin — 2018-02-19T23:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-235140>

My PCP said that my ferritin levels were not high enough for direct treatment. I will pursue using milk thistle, green tea and avoid the use of cast iron cookware. Have started to ramp up on CT. Water from the tap is in the forties this time of year. Get morning sun every day it shines, sometime many hours.

However, the reason for the blood testing originally was because over the last few months I have had persistent pressure (not pain) in the upper abdomen under the diaphragm in the center and slightly to the right. The doc thought this was probably the gall bladder. I am sure that the mention of a high saturated fat diet this time of year did not help. I went through upper right quadrant ultrasound imaging. They did not see anything remarkable in any organ in that region. Then I had a HIDA scan to test the gall bladder function. This showed 75% emptying which was quite normal.

Of course, the next step is to do a colonoscopy and abdominal CT scan (mega X-rays). I have not committed to either one at this point.

The possible causes of this pressure could be any number of things from bad to transitional. I have weird proprioception nerve issues mainly in extremities that have been around for 9 yrs. Years of heavy fruit consumption probably. This has not been debilitating just annoying. Maybe though the nerves in the upper abdomen are giving weird signals now. It feels like a big knot. The muscles are strong but bending there is not normal. Otherwise, my abdomen is flat and toned.

### caroline — 2018-02-20T08:00:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-235172>

just saying "Hi" Jonathan ....following

### Jonathin — 2018-03-07T01:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-236517>

I am think that this gut pressure issue maybe related to a general issue for me about proper blood flow regulation and a degree of swelling in hands and feet. Also I can get a strong NO effect noticeable in biceps with modest air cold exposure outside. Cold baths not so noticeable. I can massage my belly in the sun after CT and there is nothing extraordinary that I feel. Why to this degree and why now? Last fall I was doing plenty of hard work with a pitchfork turning compost and digging substantial holes in the ground. I did not have this then. Did I pull or strain something? I sure don't remember doing that and these types of activities are not new to me. Otherwise, my digestion and BMs seem ok. Just moderate discomfort when bending, twisting, etc (not pain). Seems to increase somewhat with drinking cold water or eating modest amounts of food. I do have a endoscopy scheduled in 2 wks but am wondering if a more complete ultrasound beyond the upper right quad might show something more such as misplacement of the stomach, etc even tho I don't sense anything there. I really want to avoid a CT scan due to the high radiation hazard. MRI is not an option with insurance. My fear is that none of this may show anything conclusive. I have heard a few stories about people's gut aches and pains reaching no conclusion with medical docs.

### Jonathin — 2018-09-03T04:57:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-250006>

Less than 2 months ago my wife died from the complications of multiple myeloma, a bone marrow cancer. The date of her death was less than 8 weeks after her diagnosis in the ER. Her blood tests were off the chart on many parameters. By the time she died she become highly emaciated as she chose to forego any medical treatment and ended up discharging every bit of her stored energy. She vomited 100’s of times. It was truly bad. She slept a lot which was odd because she generally had lousy sleep for the last twenty years. She did have pain but fortunately it was not severe.

Obviously, this stressed me greatly. Caring for her and watching her fade so quickly was dramatic and traumatic. Jack said I was lucky to only loose 10% of my body weight. Family and friends and hospice could only do so much for her or me. What they could do was greatly appreciated though. Perhaps it was a good for everyone that her dying process did not go on longer. I was exhausted at the end. I've never been so tapped.

The reason that I am writing this is that I know other people in our community have experienced the death of a spouse (or child) but probably most have not. It seems like this is one of the most traumatic events you can go through. Stress can come from many sources but for most of us being close to intimate death near us is probably the top of the list. How one deals with it literally shapes the remaining life you have. I am in my 60's so I have to see this as an opportunity to recover and rebuild my life but only with concerted effort based on some sort of personal plan or strategy.

I used every oppurtunity I could think of to be in nature a la Jack Kruse especially during the roughest time. Every morning I did grounding, watched the sun rise and followed circadian cycle as much as possible. But of course my emotional and mental energy was being dissipated quickly at the same time. The flower pot had many holes in it. My dopamine social network became extremely important to me. How I related to those important people in my life became more important to me than ever. I am not a highly social person by nature but I did not hold back. Some people I had known but did not considered part of my list, quite unexpectedly rose in rank. Our families do not live nearby but they all came once and sometime twice in those few weeks. Some local friends became super friends. My gratitude is great as is my strenghtened connection to them.

My wife thought she made good choices just as I think I do now. Making some good choices may not be enough if you want to avoid suffering though. Sometimes unexpected shit happens. I did not see this coming. Perhaps she sensed it a number of months before. On the other hand, poor choices will often make for bad outcomes. Being educated, thoughtful and making better choices might help us avoid these extremes of suffering. I see that as the main purpose of the Jack Kruse community. Reinventing your body and your life has endless possibilities if you remain focused. What we want and how far we are willing to go in our life is an open book.

### Inger — 2018-09-03T17:02:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-250038>

I am so sorry for your loss Jonathin.

I cannot even imagine how painful it must have been.
Being connected with the wild nature as much as I can has helped me huge every time when I go through pain, even if I have never faced anything like that.
You are so lucky to have dear friends and love around you!

### Inger — 2018-09-03T17:04:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-250039>

I wonder if she did not feel anything before the diagnosis? Like was she tired often, could she sleep, etc.?

Are there no warning signs, before one get cancer?
Or are we just so good in ignoring them?

### drezy — 2018-09-03T17:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-250047>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=250006)
>
> Less than 2 months ago my wife died from the complications of multiple myeloma, a bone marrow cancer. The date of her death was less than 8 weeks after her diagnosis in the ER. Her blood tests were off the chart on many parameters. By the time she died she become highly emaciated as she chose to forego any medical treatment and ended up discharging every bit of her stored energy. She vomited 100’s of times. It was truly bad. She slept a lot which was odd because she generally had lousy sleep for the last twenty years. She did have pain but fortunately it was not severe.
>
> Obviously, this stressed me greatly. Caring for her and watching her fade so quickly was dramatic and traumatic. Jack said I was lucky to only loose 10% of my body weight. Family and friends and hospice could only do so much for her or me. What they could do was greatly appreciated though. Perhaps it was a good for everyone that her dying process did not go on longer. I was exhausted at the end. I've never been so tapped.
>
> The reason that I am writing this is that I know other people in our community have experienced the death of a spouse (or child) but probably most have not. It seems like this is one of the most traumatic events you can go through. Stress can come from many sources but for most of us being close to intimate death near us is probably the top of the list. How one deals with it literally shapes the remaining life you have. I am in my 60's so I have to see this as an opportunity to recover and rebuild my life but only with concerted effort based on some sort of personal plan or strategy.
>
> I used every oppurtunity I could think of to be in nature a la Jack Kruse especially during the roughest time. Every morning I did grounding, watched the sun rise and followed circadian cycle as much as possible. But of course my emotional and mental energy was being dissipated quickly at the same time. The flower pot had many holes in it. My dopamine social network became extremely important to me. How I related to those important people in my life became more important to me than ever. I am not a highly social person by nature but I did not hold back. Some people I had known but did not considered part of my list, quite unexpectedly rose in rank. Our families do not live nearby but they all came once and sometime twice in those few weeks. Some local friends became super friends. My gratitude is great as is my strenghtened connection to them.
>
> My wife thought she made good choices just as I think I do now. Making some good choices may not be enough if you want to avoid suffering though. Sometimes unexpected shit happens. I did not see this coming. Perhaps she sensed it a number of months before. On the other hand, poor choices will often make for bad outcomes. Being educated, thoughtful and making better choices might help us avoid these extremes of suffering. I see that as the main purpose of the Jack Kruse community. Reinventing your body and your life has endless possibilities if you remain focused. What we want and how far we are willing to go in our life is an open book.

Thanks for sharing this.

### Jonathin — 2018-09-03T17:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-250048>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=250039)
>
> I wonder if she did not feel anything before the diagnosis? Like was she tired often, could she sleep, etc.?
>
> Are there no warning signs, before one get cancer?
> Or are we just so good in ignoring them?

I think the later. Denial can be subtle enough to largely be ignored by the conscious mind. Looking back I think she knew on some level that she was declining. The scary thing is that I see some similar signs in other people of my age group that I am close to and I am uncomfortable about their resignation. I am inclined to resist my aging process as much as possible.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-deuterium-levels-best-proxy-for-heteroplasmy.20764/post-233507>

As of 2015, Boros was investigating the possibility that Deuterium transfer to fat may be the best marker of mitochondrial failure -- is this currently accepted in Epi-Paleo land as the best proy for heteroplasmy?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-26T14:32:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-deuterium-levels-best-proxy-for-heteroplasmy.20764/post-233533>

As time elapses from birth one thing is clear.....mitochondrial likes DDW more. The higher the metabolic rate of the tissue the more DDW that tissue requires. I don't think the fractionation testing as it stands is a good measure. I have another test in mind as a proxy

### JanSz — 2018-01-26T16:33:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-deuterium-levels-best-proxy-for-heteroplasmy.20764/post-233548>

\=====================

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=233533)
>
> As time elapses from birth one thing is clear.....mitochondrial likes DDW more. The higher the metabolic rate of the tissue the more DDW that tissue requires.

Does that mean that content of D in matrix water varies?
How much?
\------------
Metabolic water∼115 ppm (D/H)
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/deuterium-in-foods.20423/page-7#post-229480](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/deuterium-in-foods.20423/page-7#post-229480)
[http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/74/19\_Supplement/1426](http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/74/19_Supplement/1426)
names there László G. Boros, Gábor Somlyai
Metabolic water by complete fat oxidation, coupled with cytochrome-c, contains low average ∼115 ppm (parts per million) deuterium due to deuterium discrimination by plant lipogenic enzymes during photosynthesis.

### Melisa — 2018-01-27T02:40:59+0200

Hello I'm 44 year old female diagnosed with endometrial cancer. A week ago I had a complete hysterectomy. The DR believes it was all contained in the uterus with no spread to the lymph nodes. I've been practicing IF and Keto for over a year, work out 4 times a week and take supplements ( D3, Mag, Adrenal support, biotin, K3). I'm introducing Curcumin Supreme , B Supreme with probiotics to combat the current pain meds. I ordered Blublox glasses to help with my mitochondria going forward. Since my surgery sleep is a major issue.

Can anyone give me other advice or directions I can take to further heal and combat this cancer?

Time to biohack my body and push it.

Much appreciated.

# Thread: My Optimal Journal 2018
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 85
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alison N — 2018-01-27T05:54:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-233602>

Update to my original post:

\------------------------------
Maternal Haplotype: H1M

Paternal Haplotype: R-L51

**Family History:**

**Father:** I do not know my father’s side of anything except that he had issues with eczema.

**Mother:** Osteopenia (has had several bad car and accidents). Arthritis from nerve compression.

**Maternal Grandmother:** Rheumatoid Arthritis. She cannot tolerate any medications, painkillers, etc. It makes her very nausea. Breast Cancer – “spontaneous remission” after a year. AMD + Cataracts.

No other known diseases.

**Genetic Carrier:**

Hereditary Hematomachrosis (1 allele)

Age Related Macular Degeneration (2 alleles)

**Genetic SNPS:**

SH2B3 +/+

COMT rs4633 +/+

COMT rs4680 +/+

MAO-A +/+

CBS +/+

MTRR rs1801394 +/+

_DAO ALL +/+_

RAG1 +/+

SOCS1 +/+

_GAD ALL +/+_

PEMT +/+

TCN1 +/+

SULTA2 +/+

GSTP1 rs1695 +/-

FTO rs17817449 +/-

FTO rs9939609 +/-

FTO rs1121980 +/-

FTO rs142085 +/-

CYP2R1 +/-

CYP2R1 rs12794714 +/-

BDNF +/-

Vitamin D Binding Protein rs2282679 +/-

Vitamin D Binding Protein rs7041 +/-

FAD51 rs174548 +/-

FAD51 rs1535 +/-

PEMT +/-

CYPA12 +/-

NAT 2 +/-

BHMT +/-

MTRR rs1802509 +/-

SOD2 +/-

FOLR1 +/-

NOS1 +/-

FOXE1 +/-

Mitochondrial function:

SLC19A1 +/-

NDUFS3 +/-

COX5A +/-

COX6C +/-

SULTA1 +/-

**Bio:**

31 years old. I have lived in Portland, Oregon for the past 5 years. My husband and I moved from the MD & DE area near Ocean City, MD where we lived for 3-4 years prior. Prior to that, I grew up in MI since my early teenage years. My childhood was mostly in MD, PA & DE. I grew up taking many antibiotics (my mom is a nurse). I had two bouts of mononucleosis very young and Herpes Simplex eruptions from stress and ate the wrong foods throughout my youth. I was also a C-section delivery.

I was chubby child then lost weight by working myself out to death and trying veganism. I had a lot of energy prior to that last year and was always on the beach, swimming and out in the sun, exercising. I did eat A LOT of large fish like Albacore Tuna. Eventually, I experimented with Veganism for a year and around this time I got sick (was also working three jobs) The last 6-months of living on the East Coast we lived in a musty house near the water that had visible mold and I experienced a lot of emotional stress. From this point into the first year of living in Portland, I felt the worst.

I have had a low thyroid since 2003-2004 but never took medication for it. Hashimoto’s with antibodies around 33 (resolved autoimmunity with diet)

I also had an ATV accident in 2005ish and had a concussion. A few years later, it revealed mild to moderate degenerative disc disease and a small syrinx that is stable in my thoracic spine. I experience a lot of pain from this. I also had two other car accidents that caused cervical stiffening and shoulder pain. A brain MRI in 2009 showed a 0.3cm “white matter lesion.” Wanting a functional MRI follow-up.

Originally **DX’d with Mercury Poisoning** (Chris Shade’s lab had to extend their own graph because limits were over them). Removed three mercury amalgam fillings with a biological dentist. During this time, I had neuropathy and my entire right leg would go numb walking down the street. I successfully chelated to very low levels over 1-2 years. Then chronic fatigue set in, had all the **positive mold labs** including the HLA-mold-susceptible genotype. **Also positive for Lyme (2015)**. I moved in 2016 from one very bad apartment (within 15 minutes I would be completely stupefied in that environment) to a new one.

_Over 2014-2016 I’ve tried various treatments but nothing worked except ozone (rectal) therapy – which turned the lights back on in a big way, but after a few weeks, the effects fizzled out (environment sucked). It also became incredibly expensive. I have stopped taking any supplements at this point because I am focusing on the environment and Jacks’ recommendations. Vitamin D level has been between 33-40 consistently. I felt the best over the summer in 2017 as I began spend WAY more time in the sun._

**Current Symptoms:**

Numbness and tingling in hands and feet. I went to the ER in November 2017 for an episode of total left hand to elbow numbness but the EKG was normal (did not run labs) and the doc mentioned it sounded like MS to him. Ironically, I was standing at my un-grounded and unprotected laptop at the time.

· Brain fog, fatigue, anxiety.
· On-going and intermittent periods of apathy and lethargy, intermixed with days of great enthusiasm and energy.
· IBS – had constipation forever, which after introducing Iodine caused tremendous loose stools, which has since stabilized between the two.
· Eczema on my right hand only (gets better with Megaspore probiotics) – recently just resolved a bit after eating a lot of nuts and peanut butter (which I NEVER usually eat), but the cravings were intense. It still primarily itches at nighttime.
· Cold hands and feet.
· I occasionally get “lightning strike” like whips of pain across my torso – completely random.
· Dry and itchy eyes at night – sometimes tearing.

**Home + Work Situation:**

Third floor apartment. Feeling better after moving out but still significant issues. Live with spouse who snores, loudly, all the time. I have chronically disturbed sleep and tend to awaken 12:00 am – 2:00 am nightly to switch rooms. This is not because I have trouble staying asleep but the noise wakes me up. Interestingly, on vacation, he sometimes will not snore at all.

I’ve turned off the WIFI on my laptop and the WIFI on our Frontier router isn’t emitting a signal whenever I’m home; however, my husband turns it on because he’s addicted to his cellphone and manages lots of people remotely.. (Despite me trying to educate him). When it is on, I am on the other side of the room. It is in the range of 0.30-0.1. In the bedroom, it is 0.1-0.05. My phone is on airplane mode 90% of the time.

Body voltage measurements in the bedroom is .887 – 1.33 and floats around 1.02 with the bed pulled from the wall. We have never had any lamps plugged in nor any electronics while we sleep. We have the breaker panel in the bedroom but after turning them all off and back on, the electric readings jump from 3.445 so they stay on. I primarily reside in my apartment where the electric levels are lowest at 0.445 – 0.673 and the RF I cannot get below 0.10. It is USUALLY at .30 most of the time.

I have not yet measured any magnetic frequencies.

I have a BioMat but body voltage measures over 3.025 so I don’t use it anymore – and it makes me very tired after (redox is shot).

I work at a Fertility Clinic and I work on a computer for 10 hours – three days a week and half a day on Saturdays. The last time I measured, it was 0.30 and now it is through the roof 3 - 6.00 – the levels change according to how my co-workers use their cell phones… so my job is killing me…. WOW.. I will be trying to my hardest to figure out a new income stream so I can quit.

I do use Indoor Bluetech glasses the entire day I work, when I’m lucky to work on the third floor we have a huge glass window that gives natural light (though it is UV tinted) the EMF’s aren’t quite as bad. I use the orange Uvex and a special made BPI 550 tinted lense together as soon as the sun goes down.

**Diet:**

I am now recently following the Leptin RX more strictly. No grains, beans, processed sugars or heavy carbohydrates. I am always craving high fat foods, love bacon, pork, beef (grassfed). I love seafood and get wild and fresh when I can but it’s mostly canned Wild Salmon, smoked oysters (and raw), canned sardines, clams and frozen wild scallops and octopus occasionally. I have pea protein powder and exogenous ketone powder on hand – mostly because I can’t always afford the best food.

I also eat a ton of coconut products but have tried to hold off for summer months. I buy many green leafy vegetables from the Asian markets, but I am guessing this is a circadian mismatch because gai-lin & bokchoy definitely do not grow indigenously in Oregon…. **Would these be okay in the summer, despite the fact they do not grow where I am?** _Jack mentioned he eats coconut in the summer months, but there not native to New Orleans either…._

I just started add duck fat to my coffee and it is surprisingly good.

I eat many alternative yogurts like coconut, almond and cashew as I cannot do dairy but I love yogurt and it seems to be a good source of cultured food. I also use a lactic acid ferment to make my own kraut.

I drink a lot of green tea of various types – I’ve been boiling each batch for 90 seconds as Jack recommends but I drink about 40 oz. of tea a day.. I just crave the tea.. I drink coffee occasionally.

I have a counter-top RO water machine (AquaTru) and I add minerals to it.

Constantly craving sea salt.

Constantly craving nuts and nut butters.

Always thirsty despite drinking 65-80 oz. of filtered water a day.

**Allergies:**

Gold

Shrimp (acute allergy)

Chicken eggs (duck eggs are good)

Wheat

Dairy – would like to experiment with the cultured ghee but afraid it might cause autoimmune issues – molecular mimicry?

**Goals:**

Find a competent doctor to assist in progress towards optimal.

Fix REDOX.

Heal leaky gut and increase diversity of flora.

I weigh approximately 145-150 lbs. I would like to use 15-20 lbs.

Gain muscle in upper back.

**Protocols:**

Following the Leptin RX – doing CT about 3-4x a week. My bath tub in the winter is 52-57 degrees so I don’t add ice. I am working up from 10-15 minutes to longer. My energy sharply increases when I do it – sometimes I cannot sleep because my mind gets very racey. I always shiver - If I do it in the AM, I do actually get hungry. (Jack said it usually diminishes hunger).

Implementing the Mitochondrial RX and Osteoporosis RX soon.

My biggest hardship is financial and needing to wait to get labs drawn, etc – things move more slowly than I would like

Will be posting updated labs soon.

Last BUN/Creatinine Ratio: 14

TSH: 5.3

FT4: 1.0

FT3: 2.0

\--------------

**QUESTIONS
**
For the Leptin RX - Is it okay to eat within 30 minutes of rising if it is technically 5:30-6 am and it is still dark? Or is it better to wait until the sun rises?

> _I live on the third floor of an apartment building. I’ve turned off our WIFI router (occasionally its on because of husband) but when it is I’m the farthest away. The lowest I can get it to on the Accousticom meter is a fluctuation between 0.30-0.10. I’ll usually sit on the floor that measures the lowest part of the house. ELF electric frequencies measure .9-1.3 in the bedroom. I’ve pulled the bed as far away as the wall as I can and it’s still between these levels — it changes depending if neighbors are using their washing machines, etc. Husband snores… so I move to the living room floor which electrically, measures .5-.6 volts.. lower than actual bed area. Everything around me in unplugged. I find I can actually dream when I sleep on the floor in living area- however, I’m CHRONICALLY interrupted at 12-1:30 am to move from my bed to somewhere else.. then I have a routine of turning off WIFI,, silencing his cell phones, making the “bed” .. so on. Not optimal, but not sure what else to do? >

I have indoor blue techs while I work and use blue blocking glasses as soon as the sun goes down - I’ve been doing this since August 2017 - EMFs where I sit at work measure 0.3 - 1. I have yet to measure electronic frequencies nor have I yet measured magnetic.. (to come)…_
>
> _I had mononucleosis twice in high school - I grew up eating SAD - fast food, allergenic foods - worked out like crazy in high school, lost fat, but then went vegan. Lost menstrual cycles.
>
> Fast forward, 20 - 25 years old I lived in Ocean City, MD - was able to work three jobs, slept probably 4 hours a night - had great energy, but I was always on the beach, in the sun, in the ocean, working out.. Never knew where I got the energy from and how I could be as active as I WAS … after 2 years of cycling through that.. we moved into apartment that had visible black mold and smelt “damp” and musty.. after a summer in that house, things got weird and stressful… so we moved to the west coast._
>
> _Apartment living - diagnosed with mold toxicity - very moldy apartment - I diagnosed myself with Lyme via urine PCR - quit all doctors because their advice wasn’t working. Then I stumbled upon Dr. Kruse’ work and I’ve been implementing it ever since._
>
> _As of 2017 - the #1 thing that helped me the most has been Iodine… I’m taking 87.5 mg right now. I eat seafood, crave fats, ketogenic diet & hardly any sugar. I follow a seasonal eating approach… the vitamin D / sunshine is an issue right now…. not sure whether to invest in the Fiji Sperti Lamp or temporarily tan.. or just wait it out until Summer…_
>
> _My next step is implementing the Mitochondrial RX but wondering if my nnEMF environment is too high for it to be useful or futile? Our lease ends in October 2018 but financially speaking, not sure what we’ll do._
>
> _In school to become a scientist, have always been interested in health, but money is a real issue. Things move VERY slowly health wise because of this._
>
>
> **_DIAGNOSES_**
>
> _DDD: Thoracic Spine. Cervical Spine: loss of lordosis and multiple whiplash car accidents._
>

>

I had an ATV accident - hit a tree at 30 mph and flipped off landing on my stomach - lost consciousness for about 15 seconds; never saw a doctor being a dumb teenager. Brain fog has increased exponentially since then. Numbness/tingling in hands/feet. Some say its neuropathy from disc compression and others say its a progression towards MS._
>

>

>
> _The only supplement I’m on is Iodine with Selenium, Magnesium, C, Zinc, B - the co-factors, that’s about it._
>
>

>
> _As of today, I seem to have issues with “good days” and “bad days” - One day I have great enthusiasm and energy the next day I’m fried and apathetic. I could easily sleep 12-14 hours on those days. Poor exercise endurance._
>
> _I have a lot of GI issues - I know there’s leaky gut and dysbiosis. Just submitted a Urine Organic Acid.. wanted to perform a Viome; anyone here do that yet? Will be working on repairing gut with specific pre and probiotics. Recently went to the Philippines and haven’t quite felt the same since… Most likely picked up a protozoa from freshwater. I had strange rashes after several swimming incidents._
>
> _Next steps are implanting the Mitochondrial RX - I’m still listening to 2012, 2013 webinars (I know, lots to catch up on)._
>
> _I’mw working on building up muscle to stabilize my back and reduce pain. I’m trying to best to mitigate the nnEMF situation. Ideally quit my job and move out of this apartment but in the interim, I’m either outside in the park reading or in the apartment in the lowest radiation area)._
>
> _I have high C4A and TGFB1 - it seems to go down with Zeolite intake._
>

### JanSz — 2018-01-28T15:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-233671>

When able you may want to check your body's (breath condensate) for deuterium.
Desirable is to be under 130ppm (D/H)

Eat lots of fat, pork and beef lard, butter, coconut oil they will lower your deuterium.
Egg yolks are low in deuterium
Do not drink coconut water it is high in deuterium.
Solids in the green salads you eat are low in deuterium, but juice made of it is high.

Aquafina made by Pepsi is the only known to me RO water that was tested and found low in deuterium (139.5ppp (D/H), that is much much lower than any known to me natural waters (that usually are very expensive).
I have send for testing RO water sold by Costco, will publish results when get them. That water is the cheapest that I know of.

Body makes matrix water out of the fat you eat. 1 volume of fat make 1.1 volume of water.
When you eat enough fat your need for drinking water should be less.
Think of 1.5 -2 liters/day

Eat Redmont salt, at least 2 teaspoons a day (but no more than 12 tablespoons (twelve Tbsp).
It eases kidneys energy use, and adjusts positively many micronutrients.
Body, when adjusting to low available salt recirculates existing salt, that makes kidneys work overtime.
Up to 60% energy used by kidneys may be spend on that work.

KIE of D to O, N, and C.
(hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, carbon)
(less common) isotopes are used by our bodies to adjust biological processes
